text:
Pho, summer rolls, iced coffee, vermicelli, broken rice plates, all the staples in a quick-service format. Mid-priced and great for lunch. Come right when they open at 11:30 to beat the lunch rush.

New digs with a clean restroom. For now.

OK, the hype about having Hatch chili in your burger is overrated. It's OK. I do like the kick. But it's an average burger. Good bun. Good fries. I rather go to In-N-Out or Freddy's. But you try it and see if it is your cup of tea.

5* = A top favorite. Mind-blowing good. I will definitely return and I will go out of my way to go here. Worth every dime of my money.

4* = Outstanding. Tucson-top-tier. Some elements not quite as good as it gets, but it still is worthy of my regular business.

3* = Good experience. I might do repeat business if it is convenient, the only option, the only one in its category, or on a whim, but would not be among the first thing to come to mind. Would definitely recommend you trying it to get your own take on it.

2* = Not so good. Would not recommend at all. Waste of money.

1* = How are they still in business? Stay away at all costs!

ETA: I spoke with Angie, the manager, and she was wonderful. Although the customer service we experienced during our dinner was not great, I feel like Angie heard and validated my frustrations.  She offered me a gift card to return to the restaurant and I look forward to a more positive experience next time.

I have food allergies and came to Timpano thinking they would not have an issue accommodating.  Unfortunately, I found that the majority of their items could not be made without dairy, soy, and nuts.  Our server took our order and returned a LONG while later to tell me they couldn't make the dishes he had told me would be OK.  He suggested I just order a protein and steamed vegetables (which is super boring and not what I want when I go out to eat). We were able to enjoy the mussels, thankfully. We decided to share scallops and broccolini. Our server explained that it typically comes with 3 scallops so he added 1 scallop on so we could each have 2.  Imagine my SHOCK to receive a bill for $45 for 4 scallops and 4 sprigs of broccolini - a $15 charge for 1 scallop (when the only side they could give me was a vegetable).  We were there for over 2 hours (not because we were having a leisurely meal, but because it took them WAY too long...) and didn't want to get into a battle over the bill then.  I have tried to reach out to the restaurant to give them an opportunity to listen to my concerns, but have not received a response.  There are too many good restaurants that care about customer service to return here.

Jones is a long-running comfort food restaurant in Center City Philadelphia.  It's décor is '50s American retro stylish (or I think that's what it is.)  Anyway it looks cool and the fireplaces add to its warmth. The food here is traditional American dishes such as meat loaf and pot pie or a Thanksgiving dinner served every day.  As this is a Stephen Starr restaurant, prices are a bit above what one thinks of paying for these comfort foods but they are prepared well in an engaging atmosphere....and the serving sizes are ample.  Recently I met a friend here for dinner and we had a really good experience.  We started out with an order of zucchini frites which I liked more than my friend did and I wound up taking some home with me to enjoy later.  Our main courses were excellent.  My friend got a roasted salmon platter and I got the huge but tasty turkey pot pie.  The pot pie's puff pastry crust was flaky and perfectly prepared and the turkey and vegetables in a cream sauce was very warming on a winter's night.  We had a very personable waitress and left Jones felling well-satisfied.

The review is for the canele. It's chewy with thick outer crust. It's not too sweet but it's missing the citrus and rum flavor.

As always service is great

My husband loves Hibachi, and my kids are getting to the age where they can appreciate the show, so we decided to check out Kuchi. 

Overall, our experience was good. Our chef was actually Japanese, which made this the first time we had a Japanese hibachi chef in Florida. He was hilarious, squirting sake into the adults' mouths and water into my son's face (to his delight). They were very generous with firing up the grill -- I'm relieved we left with eyebrows still intact. The kids loved it!

Service was a little slow. It appeared that one waitress handled all the orders, drinks, salad, and soup for the whole Hibachi room. It was fine, except that I probably would have ordered another bottle of sake if she'd been able to come back and ask if I wanted anything else. Oh well, their loss. The sake they serve is not great quality, so I probably avoided a hangover by not getting more. My husband was not offered another bottle of beer, and the water was slow to be replenished. I left very thirsty.

The food was fine. It was pretty standard Hibachi. My husband and I both had the steak and shrimp combo. My husband added on a side of lobster. My daughter had the kid shrimp meal, my son had the kid chicken meal. The kids loved the noodles and the veggies. We all felt that the salad was too creamy, could have been more acidic.

We'll probably return again for Hibachi once I recover from the salt bomb. This certainly was the best Hibachi I've had in the Tampa area. And I'll probably order two beers for myself to avoid dehydration during the salty meal.

We had such a nice date night at the US premiere of '100 Words For Snow' in this amazing, intimate theater, courtesy of Yelp. Besides the production, starring one fantastic actress, the theater itself, with only three sections of seating, gathered in a round manor was so unique!! It reminded me of Shakespeare's globe theater in London, and I couldn't have loved it more! 

The sound was wonderful, views were fantastic, even from the back row, which we chose. (No assigned seats!) 
* I thought the designated seats for late comers was a super smart idea too. 

The ask because of how small the space is to not just silence your phones, but turn them off as the actors can feel vibrations. How crazy to think! 

The beer offerings were unique and I enjoyed the fact that they offered hot chocolate with marshmallows, which matched the theme of the play, for a small donation. 

The theater is located on the ground floor of The Drake,  but the entrance is actually on Hicks Street. I never knew  this existed, but let me tell you, it is now on my radar. It's really a gem! We couldn't have enjoyed the production or the cozy vibes here any more! Excellent experience! We will be back for sure!

I've been wanting to try axe throwing for over a year! With a Groupon in hand, a bottle of wine and good friends - we finally checked out Bury The Hatchet on Friday. (Please note, even with the Groupon there is a $12 booking fee online)

I was definitely a little worried about the experience, given that a) I'm a clumsy person what could go wrong, and b) that scary viral video many of us have seen.. if you don't - honestly don't worry about it unless you want to look it up which may deter you from the experience. 

I could not have been happier with our experience. First off - they are BYOB (no hard liquor) and allow you to bring in your own food. BYOfood also means you're allowed to get delivery to the location!

The experience starts off with a bit of safety, and training with an instructor. Our instructor Taylor was fabulous - he always made me feel safe, gave great tips, and was very personable and fun during the 2 hours we spent with him. After the training and practice sesh, he grouped us with another group of 4 - and we had 2 fun friendly games of competition. One was King/Queen of the Hill which reminds me of "knockout" in basketball , and the 2nd was a bracket tournament where you throw I think 6 or 10 times to get your seed. The games were so much fun, they're as competitive as your group makes them - and great for adding another element to throwing axes!

Afterwards you are allowed to hang out and play cards, connect 4, jenga or any of the other games they have laying around.

The 2 hours flew by, much faster than I thought! I would definitely recommend axe throwing as a fun experience to try, and would recommend this location based on our experience!

It would be easy to walk by Rowhouse Grocery and not even realize it .... but especially if it is Tuesday afternoon you do not want to do that.  You see on Tuesdays, Rowhouse bakes Pulla - the wonderful bread pastry from Finland.  If you are like me, you will want to get your fix of Pulla because I only seem to get to Helsinki every few years and though I always have some Pulla to take with me when I leave Finland, it's gone in a couple of days.  This cardamom infused sweet bread goes naturally with coffee or tea ... but as a Finnish friend in Vienna taught me, you can also make a tasty sandwich with it.  Anyway, Rowhouse Grocery has fresh out-of-the-oven Pulla every Tuesday afternoon.  Get there early or reserve ahead of time.

p.s.  They also make a killer orange upside-down cake.  If they have this orange treat when you stop in, def add it to your order.
Friends of mine were hosting a small bazzar to give back to a community of cafts people in developing worlds.  The place allowed them to do so on their patio. That gets big marks from me.

Brawleys is my kinda eats! Been here a couple of times now this summer of 2019 and loved it. I had an amazing omelette, and the 4 items menu is superb and you can pick and choose what you want on it for a super low price. Heck, everything here is affordable and the food is plentiful. And let's talk about the toast- the toast is thick and dense and good. Coffee is yummy and poured constantly. Great view of the mountains north of Wrightstown make this a beautiful place to enjoy and gaze out. One of these days I may try the lunch, but breakfast is soooooooooo good!

Good spot in Ybor. They had karaoke and everyone seemed to be having a great time, the crowd was really diverse as well. The bar upstairs is the easiest spot to get a quick drink. It's about what you expect for Ybor City. I think this place captures Tampa pretty well.

Solid tacos for a fair price, especially in center city. 

Tacodelphia is located across the street from the Suzanne Roberts Theatre Company on South Broad street. Their tacos come with 3 per order, all at the same price and you can mix and match anything except for shrimp which is a great bonus. My partner and I both got the same thing - 2 chicken and 1 veggie. This comes with any toppings and salsas you want - again you can mix and match any of these toppings or salsas. 

The tacos are good, but I agree with the reviewer that stated they're not much different than tacos I make at home. My takeaway is that it's a solid spot to get your fix, but nothing that drives it above and beyond. 

They also have house made horchata which is a welcome bonus! 

However it's noteable that they do not have any public restrooms for use, even for customers. No complimentary tap water - bottled water only.

Good and friendly service.

I bought my phone with the screen guard protection plan. Meaning that if I chip or crack the screen guard, T-mobile will replace it for free. Unfortunately, my phone has been discontinued, so the stores have stopped ordering new screen guards for my model. That means that some stores will not carry what I need.

At this location, not only did they still have some of the screen guards left, but the employee who helped me said that if they do run out, they will order one shipped to me for free since I have the plan. When I've been to other T-mobile locations (International Plaza, cough cough), they just said they were out and did not offer a solution to me.

Plenty of parking outside, facility seems clean, employees all wearing masks correctly.

This review is for Restaurant Week only - see my previous experience and review.

My partner and I brought my future in-laws to R2L on Friday during Restaurant Week during their visit to Philadelphia for the beautiful views, and the food and service I had raved about. The views, again were spectacular - but this time around the service and food left a bit to be desired. 

Understandably so, restaurant week had the kitchen and waitstaff slammed. Service was professional, but a bit slow - and unfortunately that was the most memorable part of the service. What I mean to say is that, our server probably said 20 words to us all night, didn't ask if we had any questions about the menu, or talked about his favorite menu items, cocktails etc. In short, there was basically no conversation whatsoever which I found a bit surprising.

Next there was a bit of a mix up in the kitchen. One of my party ordered her salmon very very rare. 3 of the 4 of our party ordered the salmon. The server didn't ask anything about my salmon, but I assumed it would be ordered as the chef recommends since I didn't ask for anything special. However, the server did ask my partner how he wanted his salmon - he had it as chef recommends. When the salmon was served, mine was raw. Unfortunately I had to send that back - and they did apologize for the mix-up, but thought this was worth mentioning. The salmon was underwhelming, not at all my previous experience of one of the best salmon I'd had in my life. This one was a tad over cooked and under seasoned. 

We got an order of the lobster mac and cheese, which is still divine and I highly recommend that dish. However the portion was much smaller than I remember, I'm not sure if that's my memory playing tricks on me or not though.

Overall, based on my experience I would probably avoid Restaurant Week like the plague, but don't let this experience prevent you from trying R2l. I do think it's worth it for the view - but I do think you pay more for the view, than the quality of food compared to a lot of gems in the city.

had such a wonderful night at this quaint cozy BYOB!

it's perfect distance from Bok Bar as well so we went there first for some pre-dinner drinks and came here for a date night dinner.

we started with the escargot, gnocchi and fried oysters. i'm not a huge fan of escargot but my boyfriend said it was scrumptious! i dipped my bread in the creamy sauce it came in and i was blown away, so much flavor. the gnocchi was cooked perfectly, little cloud pillows of deliciousness. the fried oysters OH the fried oysters, SOOO GOOD. i never thought that scrambled eggs could taste so good especially with fried oysters. these apps alone are definitely a must try!

my boyfriend and i split the rib eye and it was cooked PERFECTLY and the fries were amazing but i was so full from the steak. 

all in all, this restaurant is a gem. great food, service and BYOB. what more do you need?

It was a little spicy for me, but the amount of food I got for the price was amazing.

I honestly don't know what made my bowl a little spicy. I mean, I did get basically every single topping (I went a little crazy when they said I could choose as many toppings as I wanted for no extra cost, other than avocado). I'm not sure if the Umami Shoyu sauce is spicy, but next time I'll ask what has spice as I go down the line. 

The good news is it is pretty much the same person for a good chunk of the way so I wouldn't have to repeat my question too many times.

Other than the slight spice, I thought the quality of this bowl was amazing. First of all, they have kale noodles! It tasted so good that I'm guessing it can't be that healthy for you, but I haven't seen this many places. I got half kale noodles, half brown rice. 

I then went with a regular bowl and got two scoops of salmon. I topped it with almost everything, but what I remember are two types of seaweed, edamame, mango, cilantro, masago, seaweed salad, krab salad, onion flakes, and garlic crisps, with the umamai shoyu. 

It came out huge and I paid regular price. I ate the whole thing and I was stuffed. Usually I walk away from a poke place feeling only satisfied for a little bit (I can eat a ton), but this time I'm pretty sure I'll make it to dinner. That's saying a lot.

It is Chipotle style where you walk down the line to make your bowl, so it goes very fast. 

I love this place and it will be my new go to anytime I'm at the mall!

Not my favorite place, but a solid choice for the family.

My husband likes Tijuana Flats. I am not a huge Mexican/Tex-Mex fan, so it isn't my number one go to. However, we had a coupon so we decided to check it out.

I do really like the decor of these places. It's bold and fun. 

My husband got a Megajuana + Wet Burrito. It was supposed to come out smothered in queso, but they forgot. It wasn't a huge deal, since they just brought out queso in a cup. We got a Beef Quesadilla for my son. It was huge! It looks like a normal size quesadilla to me, but for a fraction of the price. I'm glad we planned for me to share that quesadilla, because there is no way my 15 month old was going to eat that. 

The actual food is just ok to me. I found the burrito kind of dry and the quesadilla was standard, nothing special. I asked my husband what he thought of the burrito and he was a huge fan. It really does show you that everyone has different tastes. 

Portion sizes are large and that is reflected in the calorie count. The calories are on the menu, which is great if you are watching your waistline, but terrible if you are watching your waistline and eating here. Just figure nothing is low calorie.

Service here is phenomenal. They actually come around and ask if everything is ok. I felt like the chips didn't have enough seasoning and they actually brought me out a side of the salt mix! I was debating going up to say something, but they made that decision for me by coming to me! It was much appreciated. 

It weirds me out that there are no garbage cans. I like to clean up after myself. I'm glad they mentioned this at the Yelp Event or else I would have spent so much time searching for the garbage! 

Overall, I think this is a good place to grab a quick bite.

Cash only, but super cheap. The syrup the use for the water ice tastes all natural and isn't chalk full of artificial stuff like other places. There isn't really an area to sit down outside aside from a bench. Overall, a nice summer treat.

Really friendly lady who helped me. I always get the delicious almond croissant, which is fully stuffed with almond paste. The almond log has that sweet almond paste also.

Cookies are pretty good to. Oatmeal isn't overly sweet although mine was broken during transport.

If you like hazelnut or Nutella, try the marjolailne

Very prominent sushi place for the purpose of small night snack. Unfortunately during pandemic, they still don't allow dine in. I would definitely like to do that. I like this sushi place because the restaurant is good for late night snack with sake or beer. 

The food is affordable and the takeout is busy. The walk in order take about 15 minutes to prepare. We wait at the car and they deliver to your car. I got the goyoza, spicy tuna, and unagi eel. The roll at medium size. The spicy tuna with good ahi. The flavors good. The goyoza also crispy. Great snack for the night. The unagi has enough eel sauce to sweet it up. 

Overall my experience for the take is great. I will like to visit them at night when they start the dine in again.

I used to think pizzeria vetri was just okay, but I've recently went back again for take out and it was delicious. 
They have this season harvest squash pizza right now that is blowing my mind. What squash sauce is this! Amazing. Better than the other pizzas which is crazy. GET IT.
All their pizzas are delicious. Great dough/crust. Cauliflower truffle (add an egg) was wonderful and you can really taste the truffle. Crudo was very good as well, but this is not a great one for take out as the middle got a little soggy. Pepperoni is an easy win, love the fresh thyme they put on.
Tiramisu has not good, not the best I've had, but decent.
Food-wise, it's great. I haven't eaten there in a while, but they have outdoor seating when it's nice out. Service is usually friendly. And they have high chairs.

My favorite food to order here is sticky rice with mango. It's served warm. The rice isn't too sweet, the mango is fresh, juicy, and sweet.

My coworker ordered the chocolate banana spring rolls and she said it's not too salty, not too sweet, and not too flaky. Now I am curious to try it next time I order. My other coworker who ordered the pad Thai said it's delicious too.

Good bar food with a large selection to fit anyone's craving. Great happy hour and large beer selection.  We came here after hockey practice and had the room in the back reserved for us. It was perfect for the kids. There was a mini arcade that kept the kids busy while we enjoyed $5 apps from the happy hour menu. Service was great and there is plenty of room even when busy. Try the duck wings! Nice change of pace from the traditional wing

I've wanted to try Tomo for quite some time, and Vegan Restaurant Week presented the perfect opportunity. 

Covid-19 safety wise, they do a great job of spacing the tables outside and their pick up window is very convenient and safe. We opted to pick up take out and eat at home.

When I picked up the order, I thought the platter looked too small for 2 people - but I was wrong. It was a pretty perfect amount with the addition of the salad on the side. The salad with tofu was so delicious, it mimics the imitation crab meat salads and the flavor was perfect. 

I often make veg sushi at home, but Tomo blew me away. The flavors and textures are elevated to a level that I can only hope to inspire me to make at home. It's hard to choose a favorite between the shitake avocado, spicy "tuna" and the "eel" but if I had to choose - I loved the spicy "tuna" for flavor profile and texture. The shitake had a great flavor and texture, much better texture than I make at home haha. Overall I had such a great dinner and I will definitely be returning in the future!

Also, service was very nice. Alex was very kind and personable on the phone when I placed the order. All around I highly recommend you give them a shot - vegan or not!
Previously a 2 star. Had a glazed and a Bismarck. Both were decently good. Not as good as Donut Wheel Kolb & Golf Links, S. Campbell, and Valencia, but definitely a decent donut worthy of a trip.

In a gritty looking neighborhood - but one not far from increasingly cool gentrifying ones - is this storefront Laotian restaurant.  It's a gem.  Though not officially related, this restaurant is run by the daughter of the owners of the well-known Vientiane Café in University City.  The interior of the restaurant is stylish with Laotian art on the walls.  And the food is very good.  Their lunch specials are of especially great value but on a cold winter's day, nothing tastes better than their Khaopoon - aka King Soup.  This red curry broth with vermicelli rice noodles and purple cabbage, lettuce, carrots and I chose minced pork is truly tasty ... and warming ... and hearty.  Service was friendly and now I know why Vientiane Bistro is so highly rated.

This restaurant is evaluating for their food not their local brew beer. The two star was given due to after food experience. After dine their food I have to go to restroom several time. It take me a day to clear my stomach pain. Some of the food is not up to the quality. 

The restaurant do have a lot of customers. Seem like a good joint. Especially the private local brew beer. It has the brewery just next to it with tour. I did not try it. I am straight to the restaurant. 

I got the blackened rock shrimp and the Belgium Blonde. I like the Belgium blonde beer. The rock shrimp dish include the spinach fettuccine, mushroom, andouille sausage, yellow bell pepper. The sauce is creamy. The rock shrimp is firm. Not sure which item did not measured up cause stomach diarrhea. May be the sauce. Anyhow my experience is not good with the food.

Polka Deli is just one of many reasons I love living in Port Richmond. The Polish influence is real on the mean streets of the Port and it's because of fabulous places like Polka Deli. While most of their clientele seems to be Polish natives, they are very kind and hospitable to us English speaking third generationers. The lovely man behind the counter took his time with me to match me up with my kielbasa soulmate. Little known fact-Donna's bar gets their kielbasa from Polka! While the Swiaki's and Czerw's of the world get all the love and name recognition (deservedly so-they also kick ass) Polka has an equally delicious product without the hype. I wish they had fresh pierogi for sale but the frozen line they carry was pretty good. I got other delicious items from the deli including bacon, sauerkraut ,and my fave kabanosa. There's also an excellent selection of canned or jarred goods, baked goods, and candy from the Motherland. Anyone looking to do a comprehensive tour of little Poland in the city should add Polka Deli to their list!

I have to update my review now that the downstairs part is also open.

Even though it's like a third of a floor, opening the downstairs part really makes a huge difference. Now, on the first floor, there is an area for the 3 and under crowd, a huge water table play station, and a "sandy" area. 

Can we all just agree that 3 and under should stay 3 and under? This isn't the museum's fault and it probably isn't worth the money to staff someone to watch this, but there is a young baby/toddler section so they don't get run over. My son almost got jumped on by children who looked over 5 years old in the mini ball pit. All the young children were just hanging out there barely moving and some older children came jumping. Maybe they were kids with younger siblings so the parents wanted them all in the same place, but it was in no way ideal. I'm still happy that they have this little section, I just wish it was policed more. 

The "sandy" area is pretty neat. It's not really sand, but more like super tiny rubber pieces. You don't have to worry about your kids being covered in the stuff. The only reason we stayed out of that area is I'm sure my son would try to eat it. 

The giant water table is pretty cool too. They have outfit covers for kids to wear to try to stop them from getting soaking wet, but there are few and disappear quickly. If you have a splasher it might be worth bringing your own poncho. I can say that because I have a million Yelp ponchos. 

On the weekend, upstairs can be quite chaotic. The Publix is a free for all where the kids are grabbing all the goods and one museum staffer is halfheartedly trying to reshelve them. However, the other areas, although somewhat crowded, never felt overly so. 

It may be that now that my son is walking I enjoyed it more because he could explore, but I definitely think for the price this place is worth five stars.

Excellent service. It's overall a really beautifully designed restaurant. Whether you're sitting close to the Kitchen, dining room, or in the garden, it's all very intimate. The fresh baked pita is some of the best that I have had in the city, and the hummus was bangin'. We ended up going for the "A Taste of Suraya" which was $58 a person: 6 mezzas to share, a main meat or vegetable, and dessert. Although the food here is pricey, it was a very memorable experience similar to that of Zahav.

Nice. 

Clear and clean. 

Went for a short 4.5 mile (return trip inclidhike to the dam. Pleasant walk. 

Get a pass and parking is included. $20 for the year or $5 per visit. Pass works for other things such as Mt Lemon. 

A Tucson escape. So close and so beautiful. Something to be treasured and explored. There is so much to explore.

This is a decent, locally owned pizza place in the SoHo area. Nice features are easy online ordering and the ability to request curbside pickup.

We ordered a large pepperoni and mushroom pizza and a small steak and gorgonzola pizza. They had thin crust, which is how my family likes pizza. My family loved the pepperoni and mushroom pizza, and we had no leftovers. I liked the steak and gorgonzola pizza, but I'm the only one who likes gorgonzola, so it was all for me! It was only fair as a reheated product -- for whatever reason, the cheese started dripping off the sides in my toaster oven before the bottom crisped up. 

Ordering was easy online. They sent me a text saying the order was received, and a second text saying that my pickup should be delayed due to a busy kitchen. I got a third text when the pizza was "done." My only qualm about the process is that there is no designated curbside pickup parking spot, so I didn't know where to park, and that the texts did not have a link to tell the staff when I arrived, so I had to circle around a bit and then search for their phone number on my phone. There are other restaurants that have figured out curbside pickup a bit better.

TLDR: Amazing prenatal yoga classes, wonderful tribe of moms in the Mommy and Me classes, and now I can add the perfect place to throw a birthday party!

I've been going to Green Locus Yoga for close to two years now. I started when I first got pregnant and now my son is 13 months. Wow, does time fly.

Most recently, we attended a one year old birthday here. It was perfect! Here was the set up.

We ran in 15 minutes late (of course. Btw how were all the other moms on time? I honestly thought we would all be that late.) The class had already started and kids and moms were dancing on their yoga mats. I really respect places that are timely!

We had about 30 minutes of yoga/body movement to music (about 45 minutes for everyone who was on time). Then we had some refreshments the mom brought. I thought it was over at this point, but no, we had more structured play with a giant parachute! I thought it was a fantastic way to structure the birthday for one year olds - they have the attention span of squirrels so that food break was a necessity. 

At the end of the party, Mallory, the owner, told everyone to stay as long as we liked, but of course being moms of toddlers, everyone ran for nap time. However, it is a good feeling knowing you are never rushed to leave. 

After throwing Yoshi's first birthday at my house this year, this seems like the perfect alternative for his next birthday. They have a cap of 12 parents/kids (so a total of about 25), which makes sense for the space. 

We had a blast and the class was perfectly suited for the age of the kids.

Nice. 

Clear and clean. 

Went for a short 4.5 mile (return trip included) hike to the dam. Pleasant walk. 

Get a pass and parking is included. $20 for the year or $5 per visit. Pass works for other things such as Mt Lemon. 

A Tucson escape. So close and so beautiful. Something to be treasured and explored. There is so much to explore.

Such a cool fun place.  I love the creepy decorations and gothic like feel. The baristas were also dressed in costume.  Love that as well! My best friend and I decided to try something new.  We each had a kava tea and watched some live singing of Disney songs.  The tea is pretty potent. My bestie who can handle most concoctions seemed to of felt the effects of the tea for hours. It was relaxing and Very enjoyable. Will be coming back to try the other botanical teas. FYI You cannot mix the kava with alcohol. Supposedly it can make you very sick. You can mix kratom but not the kava!

We have the basic plan and use this car wash weekly.  They have a super fast checkin system and your car is clean within minutes. They have every service you can think of when it comes to cleaning your vehicle.

This place is just plain FUN.  Is it gimmicky?  Sure is, and I am here for it!

I have stopped in twice.  Once for a happy hour meal and once for late night drinks.  They get the 90s vibe right and have clever 90s names for their food and drinks.  Their food menu is fairly extensive and at happy hour there are some really good deals.  I got a cauliflower rice bowl, which was healthy AND tasty.

The bet thing about this place is all the fun games to play and the creative drinks.  They have adult Capri suns, mystery beers in paper bags, and a whole array of cocktails.  The specialty drinks are sweet but you can balance that out with a non sweet beer or glass of wine.

Over all, I like this place and the nostalgic feeling that comes along with it!

The cheesesteak pierogies are absolutely delicious with the caramelized onion on top. The staff is super friendly and welcoming. The apple sauce is great too.

Stuffed cabbage has creamy tomato sauce and well seasoned.

It's a small supermarket but carries local products such as Isgro's, High Street, Sarcobakery, Claudio's cheese. It carries wine too.?Very helpful staff. Several self checkouts with paper bags. The small shopping carts are cute .

Spinning in a dark, loud room that could be mistaken for a club is not for me apparently. With it being extremely difficult to hear the instructor and little to no instruction provided other than "we spin to the beat of the music", I didn't find it very welcoming to beginners. While not for me, I encourage you to decide for yourself as it was a packed house for a fitness center!

Pros included a killer music mix. I basically made up my own work out because I couldn't hear the directions over the music and while I could look around everyone appeared to be doing something different. The time on the bike flew by as well.

Speaking of the bike, I've spun before and know good equipment when I use it. The shower facilities were also top notch.

Cons include what I mentioned already. I come to a class to learn from an instructor, and hate not being able to follow along. I'm used to be lost or confused a bit, but I was on my own the entire class. I can use a stationary bike at another gym with my own music selection for much cheaper. I could embrace the DJ experience if the room wasn't kept pitch black 80% of the time. Also, sweat alone does not equal a good workout and the room was kept hot. I don't need the ac blasting but some cool circulation is appropriate.

Came here for dinner with a group of 6 on Friday and had a below average experience. 

To start, we all ordered the roti canai which I argue is their best thing on the menu. I could eat that curry over rice as a meal and call it a day, I highly recommend that dish.

We all ordered, including a dish for a friend that hadn't arrived yet. I ordered the Prawn Mee without pork. All the food came out at separate times. Two people had their entrees served, another 2 minutes later another 2 dishes came out, and another 3 minutes  or so the other dish came.. all but mine. However, since everything came out at different times I couldn't tell if they forgot my dish or if they were still working on it. A solid 10 minutes had gone by and my friends noticed that my entree still wasn't in front of me, but we had also been unable to get anyone's attention within that time.  Once we did, I asked about my dish - in which he went back to the kitchen. He never came back to explain the situation, so I assume they forgot the dish. Also when it came out, it had pork on it.. so double fail (see the photo). I'm not asking for a free meal, but given the experience I was surprised that the server did not address it at the very least, or offer to take my dish off the bill since it was late and caused all my friends to have to wait for me to finish, and two because it had something that I explicitly asked not to have in the meal. 

Also just to note, their menu does describe Prawn Mee as "shredded pork", but even if I ate pork - look at the photo, it's not shredded pork it's thin sliced. 

I'm still upset about it as I'm writing this review. Clearly, I will not be returning.

Went to the new location on Campbell. Very cool and trendy. Clean. Friendly staff. Had their savory bowl. Totally enjoyed it. Yay. 

Not enough parking. The lot was full and the place was not full. 



(Old review below)

The Beni. Pork belly Benedict. This visit was disappointing. Biscuit was almost not edible.  Depending on your taste, the potatoes were served along with the "the bottom of the pan crumbs". If like a lot of that you are in heaven. If you want just potatoes, well in this case there was both. 

Yum for this visit. If you notice the attached picture. I ate too fast!  Missed the before picture!  Had CAST IRON DUCK CONFIT HASH gf shallots, spinach, cabernet braised cherries goat cheese mousse, over easy eggs {12.5}. Ok. I wish there was more duck. But at the end there was a nice satisfying amount. The eggs were perfect. (Cooked easy). "Roasted" potatoes. I always fear eating them at any place, but these were not dry!  They had good flavor. The dish was fresh!  I look forward to returning. My friend had  MISSY'S MEATLOAF SANDWICHbacon, truffled arugula, tomato golden raisin ketchup, garlic aioli {9.5}.  Wow. Meatloaf bite tasted great. Seemed like there was some tarragon in the meat. It was great flavor.  Again a nice fresh flavor. Service was prompt and friendly.

I had the banana Nutella crepe. The crepe is perfect, soft with Nutella but unfortunately there are not much banana in it.

I saw the eclairs but they were so much skinnier than the last time. Thus I decided not to get it.

I got 15 percent off by showing my yelp reviews.

Suraya is a beautiful space with great food. I've only been for brunch a few times but the entire menu is great.
The drinks are amazing- the chai is excellent and so fancy.
You cannot go wrong with any flatbreads, sandwiches, and pastries.
Service is always great and they are very accommodating to those with kids.
I need to go back for dinner.

i've been here 2 times in the past and had such great experiences but i'm pretty upset about my most recent experience!

 i made a reservation to come here for my dad's birthday and made the deposit with my credit card. because of a family emergency i had to cancel (5 days before the reservation) and i called to cancel. i was notified that i wouldn't get reimbursed for the credit card hold and instead can either get a gift card or it will go towards another reservation.

i've never heard of you not being able to get your credit card deposit back from a restaurant! i was pretty upset and said what if you're not from the area and there's no way of you being able to come back to the restaurant and she had no answer for me.

basically... if you're not 100% sure that no life emergencies will happen and you may have to cancel your reservation, don't make a reservation here unless you're OK with losing $25

Philly's best gator spot hands down. My gator po boy and baguette brought me back to NOLA. 

Don't forget to check in on Yelp for 5% off.

They have breakfast options, but breakfast stops at 11am, which is unfortunate.

Becks is popular and the food is made to order so expect a wait. It's worth it though.

We've been eyeing the menu of Spice Finch for a few months now, and finally got the chance to try for last week's date night for dinner. 

Based on our experience, I would say that for a larger group it would be nice to get a bunch of dishes and share everything family style. For a couple or smaller groups I would recommend a la carte or the tasting menu. We opted to go a la carte because one of the main dishes for the tasting menu is their lamb, and we don't eat red meat. They can accommodate the dietary restrictions - but we welt like the price point wasn't worth it if we weren't getting the "main meat" attraction. 

We went all out and ordered the 2 flatbreads + 4 spreads. Their homemade flatbread is excellent - such a great texture. The berbere crisps are a nice contrast to the chewy, soft flatbread. My favorite spreads were the beet borani and the lemon yogurt which I believe was labne, if not - very similar. 

We started off with the baked cheese which I can highly recommend. The rich and savory cheese was well contrasted with the texture of the shredded phyllo - and the acidity of the tomatoes was a welcomed bright note. 

We also ordered teh Clini-Chicken Kebab which was very juicy and tender. Another well balanced dish with the swiss chard. 

Lastly we also ordered the Shakshuka and the mushroom pasta. We were a bit too full to fully enjoy the Shakshuka but it was very good, and you scoop up the goodness with more homemade flatbread. The mushroom pasta was very good, but disappointing because all the noodles were stuck together and when you bit in the middle - it tasted floury almost like when it's just barely undercooked /al dente. I took a picture so you can see what I mean. it was very good otherwise, loved the flavors of the sauce - however we didn't finish the dish because the texture/dryness of the stuck-together pasta was unpleasant to eat. 

Service was great, our server was friendly and personable and explained their tasting menu options very well. 

Between 2 people I would say you probably only need 4 dishes, the 5th is what tipped us over. 5 dishes, 4 beers tax and tip came to about $145. Overall I would say that Spice Finch is worth a visit!

Such a cute place, I'm disappointed in myself for not coming here sooner. 

I thought this was only a place where you ordered cakes for special occasions. I didn't realize you could get small desserts and coffee and eat it here! It's a really cute concept. There is very limited seating, but when we came we were the only ones in here so I think most people take stuff to go freeing up space for people who want to stay! We ended up being like most people and grabbing and going.

We went with the chocolate pound cake and a chocolate chip cookie. Both were good and satisfied my sweet tooth. The cookie was like a puck and more crunchy than soft. It had giant hunks of chocolate in it. Every bite was full of chocolate. At $3.99 it isn't an inexpensive cookie, especially because it isn't that big, but the amount of chocolate somewhat justifies the price.

The chocolate pound cake was delicious. At $5.99 that is the price I expect it to be. It was covered in a chocolate frosting. It was very moist and decadent. I almost ate the whole thing in one sitting, but I had to save some for my husband. 

Overall I like this place. I think it is cute and definitely worth stopping at if you have a sweet tooth and are in the area.

Keke's new location at USF area is less than 1 year new. We been want to visit for some time. Parking is very convenient. The line for weekend breakfast is not bad. At 8:20 am, there was no line at all. Lighting design and space is very welcoming with bright to see your food. 

We got Pina Colada with egg and bacon. The piña colada French Toast is French Toast stuff with pineapple, cream and spread with powder sugar and coconut flake. The food presentation is very nice. Colorful. The bacon is thick enough with good quality. The sunny side up egg was cook to order. Food is delicious. 

The food is a bit pricey. But it does show the potential for good food. Service definitely excellent in this place. The waiter is knowledgeable to recommend a dish.

Tight menu. Happy hour had around $1-3 knocked off of most entrees and small bites. The portions were kind of tiny, but they were extremely flavorful. Had the brussels sprouts, blue bay mussels, and pizza. Each of these are solid, and honestly we left pretty full and satisfied. Every night has a special that you can order for an entire table.

Why 3 stars? Because I had high hopes but was a bit disappointed.

   ---   ---   ---

I don't know why it took me so long to get to Zeko's, but I recently became determined to check them out soon. I happened to be in the area and figured it was finally time to make this happen.

My very first impression was that I was excited to see a drive-thru. I had been on my feet for hours and really didn't feel like getting out of the car and putting on a mask. 

My wife and I both ordered a gyro, but she went with a Greek salad as her side, while I opted for fries. To be honest, all of it was just okay. I don't love the gamey flavor of lamb and prefer gyros that only have beef, but that was not an option. As expected, I thought it was decent, but would have enjoyed it much more without the lamb. The fries were unremarkable and the salad had a minty flavor to it that I wasn't very into. We did enjoy the potato salad inside the salad, at least.

My favorite part of the meal was the banana shake. Despite the other stuff not turning out how I hoped, the shake was excellent.

Overall, I'll probably opt for some alternative options closer to my house when I'm craving Greek food in the future.

Either my tastes have changed or Smiley's has improved.  Maybe a bit of both? Regardless, Smiley's went from an option to one of my regulars over the last several months.

Getting a beef gyro is simple but delivers a wonderfully tasty lunch or dinner.  I'm hooked on their tabouli salad.  I recently tried their falafel platter.  While it doesn't replace the recently closed Mama's falafel my heart and belly miss, it does well on its own right.

I've also enjoyed a few of their real fruit smoothies.  I really love the one with honey and coconut milk, however, the name is escaping me at the moment.  Go large FTW.

They have very limited seating even prior to COVID restrictions.  I think they only have one or two tables outside, so it is more of a to-go joint.  They staff has always been friendly. Really, I can only end by saying check them out!

Solid eatery. 

Dinner choice of my friend while she visited and we were dapperfied.  

I'm sad they got rid of pork nachos, but the food is still good, still fresh. I ordered the cajun chicken nachos. The chips were fresh. There wasn't a lot of chicken on there but the other toppings were plentiful.  The drinks never stopped flowing .

Roti canai is good as usual. It's definitely a great start. Chicken satay is very tender and not over charred.

My entree of sizzling seafood combination was a disappointment. The shrimps were hard as if they're from previous day.

My husband had his usual of beef chow fun. The noodles were broken into small pieces. He's very disappointed.

came here for valentines day and had a wonderful meal!

we didn't get the combo but ordered a la carte since i only wanted the beef orders. we got the kimchi pancake, egg souffle, doenjang jigae, bibimnaengmyun, pork belly, boneless beef ribs, beef sirloin, and prime rib

the kimchi pancake was good, nothing special and same with the egg souffle. the banchans i was a little bit disappointed in, they were mostly vegetables or pickled vegetables; very simple. i really appreciate the variety of banchans at seorabol since they contain meats or protein 

the meats came out and we began to grill! all of the beef and pork was so delicious! no complaints at all. service was a bit spotty but it was v day! luckily i'm not rookie in the korean bbq game so i don't mind cooking and cutting my own meats.

the bibim naengmyun was good but something was missing to me, maybe something in the sauce or maybe i wanted the noodles to be chewier? but compared to seorabol i would say they're on the same level

the doenjang jigae was a surprise hero; it was spicy, good pork flavors and i really enjoyed it! 

all in all, it's good to have another korean bbq option in the area and i'm a fan! welcome to the neighborhood!

Overall, this was a good Dominican spot. The service was really friendly and the servers were happy to help answer any questions about the menu. We tried the ox tail, stewed goat, chicken mofongo, plantains, and their beans and rice. Everything was pretty flavorful; my only gripe was that the goat was very salty, to the point where it was pretty hard to finish. Food was also luke warm but I think it because we were near to closing time. French fries are bangin'. Their smoothie was made with fresh mango which was really solid.

Missing Charro steak big time. Our last order during Covid was a little complicated due to the time on their system not matching up with the website to place our online order. But that's nothing a quick phone call didn't fix! The charro burger and half bone in chicken were cooked to perfection. I love a simple burger and charro has all the right ingredients for a classic. Thank you Team Charro! They all work so hard!

Still some of the best coffee in the city - and still a line (that moved pretty quickly) every morning in Suburban Station.

Pricey but so much better than Dunkin or Charbucks.

The Café au Lait and Lattes (the will used your favorite type of cows or nut milk) are so delicious.  Like dessert in a cup for breakfast (and I don't even get any flavored syrups - just a plain latte or au lait is so creamy and sweet.)

Yum!!!!

Beer is fabulous!  Staff is wonderful. 

Food...ok. 5 star beer.  3 star food. Probably need to explore menu more. 

Wings were ok. 

Fish tacos were blah. That being said there was a freshness about them. Just no flavor. 

Would I go back. Most definitely. 

Place was clean.

I'm quite the fan of this place.  They have an extensive menu and the items I've always had there are great.  The restaurant itself is very large and they offer ordering at the counter and a seat yourself option if you are dining in. 

Their burritos are massive and a good deal for the price.  Their queso is one of the best, in my opinion, in the entire Tampa area.  The lines and wait can be a bit long at times, but they do also offer a drive through and online ordering.  My only gripe is the location.  I wish they had another location more centrally located in Tampa.

Capricho is breakfast and lunch place. It has Dominican food all day with Saturday special to include seafood. It located at plaza's NW corner. A lot people came here for breakfast and sandwiches because of the affordable price. 

We got the Saturday special with seafood paella and the fried meat. The fried meat is very generous. The best thing is the numbers of type meat. It includes the ham, pork lion, sausage, fried cheese, chicken and plantains. It is good to share. The seafood paella has mussels, shrimp, octopus, squid. Both dishes are good. 

Check them it if you in Temple Terrace. It is good Dominican food.

If you have never done an indoor rowing class, it's perfectly fine. The instructors here are all really great and do a good job at teaching you how to do the exercises. We did the RZ Signature which involved rowing a certain number of meters, body weight exercises, and music. Overall, it was really enjoyable and the energy was good despite being a Thursday night.

Franklin on Twiggs Eatery & Market is located off East Twiggs, West of North Franklin, in Downtown Tampa next to a cluster of small bars and restaurants including Anchor Bar, Jimmy John's Sandwich Shop, Eddie & Sam's NY Pizza, Kava Culture Kava Bar, Bavaro's Pizza, So Fresh, 1895 Kitchen, Szechuan House, Dio Modern Mediterranean, Jamaican Tropicale Jerk Hut, Taco Bus, Spain Restaurant, & Vale Food. 

Daily Lunch Specials include $2 off Mac Bowls on Mondays, $3 Tacos on Taco Tuesdays, $1 off Benny sandwich on Thursdays, & $1 off Poke Bowls on Fridays. 

I got a bunch of $3 off gift certificates at the gym that looked like orange monopoly money emphasizing their tacos beer poke & sake, so I thought I'd check this place out! 

Upon entering Franklin on Twiggs, I was immediately hit with a disturbing pungent odor that I couldn't exactly identify. The whole time I was there I was wondering what this odd odor could be from. It really made me uneasy because I'm no expert but I can only guess it could be due to either spoiled improperly stored seafood, mildew, or some other lack of cleanliness. The decor definitely looked a bit outdated with old bland dirty looking beige everywhere. On 3 of my visits in the evening this place was completely empty so I can't be the only one that can't bare the stench of this place. Maybe they get a lot of orders to go and no one realizes the smell here but people are definitely not eating in here. Until the smell problem isn't fixed I can't recommend this place. 

The Large 3 Scoop Poke bowl came with Spicy Ahi Tuna & Spicy Shrimp, Seaweed Salad, edamame, sesame seeds, crispy Onions, Asian style slaw, Cucumber, Spicy carrots, sriracha, yummy yummy sauce, and Rice. I was disappointed there was no customizability offered for toppings, there was not as many toppings available as most other poke spots, and the tuna didn't taste the freshest. 3/10 

The pulled pork Islander style taco came with shredded cabbage, Pico, cheddar jack, and Costa Rican sour cream. 6/10 

The steak spicy style taco came with shredded cabbage, cheddar jack, onion, Cilantro, jalapenos, and Spicy Rojo cream. 6/10 

I really hope management can get rid of the pungent odor at this place, paint the walls, update the decor, and provide better lighting. These improvements  would definitely go a long way to improving the feel look and smell of the place and definitely get them some more business. As it is now, I cant recommend this place.

Awesome arepas at this Colombian food truck! We spotted them on Charley Belcher's feature on Fox News because he was at In the Loop Brewing. You can find this food truck there on Wednesdays through Sundays. 

Service was friendly and helpful. Food was so good and tasty and flavorful for not a bad price. They give you a beeper and beep you when it's ready.

Meanwhile you are chilling at the outside brewery with an amazing water view enjoying life.

It's a win win! I definitely want more!

They have arepas, Colombian burgers, fried yuca and Colombian empanadas here. They also have Colombian soft drinks Colombiana and Manzana Postobon.

Check them out!!!!!!

Service is always great. Very helpful and friendly staff. You need to try pimientos stuffed with goat cheese. So addictive. Also I recommend lemon ricotta cheese, which in my opinion is more like a dessert.

Wayne Junction is usually thought of as a small neighborhood at the beginning of Northwest Philadelphia that one passes through on the train or highway.  But guess what?  There is a popular eating establishment worth getting off of the train or highway to patronize. It's called Sister Muhammad's Kitchen.  (And right around the corner in Wayne Junction there is now a new brewpub offering another dining/drinking choice.)   Of course during Covid-19 things have altered but takeout is still available and this is an establishment I wanted to try as well as patronize.

The specialty at Sister Muhammad's are their fish hoagies.  They offer two types - the standard one with a whole whiting fish ... and their famous Moslem Fish Hoagie where the fish is fried and cut into pieces. I opted for the Moslem Fish Hoagie with everything on it and am I glad I did.  It was a totally tasty sandwich bursting with flavor which I can recommend to everyone. Be hungry or in the mood to share because this is one large hoagie. Sister Muhammad's is def a place worth going a little out of your way to find.  

Tip - In non-pandemic times one can eat inside as well.

I'm so impressed with how they renovated this place. The new decor is really great! The downstairs bar had a super friendly bartender and I had a delicious glass of wine. The best part about the upstairs rooftop is that they had a great DJ but even better the music was playing and a volume where you could hear it but you could still chat.  Def check out this new, great, rooftop bar.

Ballast Point Park is at the end of the Bayshore Linear Trail in South Tampa off Interbay boulevard just north of the Tampa Yacht club and is a good spot to catch sunrises over Hillsborough Bay with a nice view of downtown Tampa. Ballast Point Park is also a good spot for fishing, holding a picnic, and for docking your boat. However, parking spots are very limited and fill up quickly because of all the people coming to fish, use the gym, attend picnics, or catch Pokémon. You can find street parking 2 blocks down the road when there are no spots left in the parks parking lot. I recommend biking or boating here if you have to.  

Ballast Point Park features:  
- a 600 foot fishing pier 
- a shaded splash park 
- playground 
- picnic areas 
- grills  
- a gazebo 
- a boat dock 
- a boat ramp  
- Taste of Boston waterfront restaurant 
- bait shop   

Shelters with grills and picnic tables are first-come, first-served and others are available by reservation. Reservations are from 8am to 6pm and must be made three days in advance and can be one year out.  

The Fishing Pier is free and open 24 hours, 7 days a week. The rest of the park is open from 6am to 8pm. The fishing pier is well lit and Florida saltwater fishing licenses are required. Equipment rental is available.  The Boat Ramp is in good condition, single lane, poured in place concrete boat ramp with boarding dock. There are only 4 spots for vehicles with trailers and parking spots fill up quickly.  

The Joe Abrahams Fitness & Wellness Center is open Monday-Friday 6 am - 8:30 pm, Saturday 8am - 3pm, and Closed Sunday. Membership is $15/month.  There's really not a lot at this park to keep me coming back other than the view of the Water and downtown which is also available on the bayshore walking trail. It would be nice if this park featured ping pong, kayak rental, a pool, or tennis courts but there are tennis courts across the street.

I am officially obsessed with rolled ice cream.

I went to another spot to try rolled ice cream, which will remain nameless, and I couldn't understand what was all the hype. My husband and I were looking for a quick date night and this place popped up. I wasn't excited about trying rolled ice cream again, but having no better idea, I acquiesced. I am so glad I did.

First of all, this place actually puts in enough mix ins for the flavor of the ice cream to change! The other place didn't put in enough so it still tasted like vanilla ice cream. Here, my husband got s'mores, and I got one with peanut butter filled pretzels and chocolate and the two rolls actually tasted different.

They take their mix ins seriously. They actually took a mini blow torch to the marshmallow to toast it! Let that sink in for a moment. 

After they roll the ice cream, you can choose unlimited toppings! I went crazy choosing a ton. My creation was absolutely delicious. I went with the bigger size and I ate all of it, plus a roll of my husband's. 

The employees are also super accommodating. Every time she drizzled something on the ice cream she asked, do you want more? This really drove home the fact that it is customizable. 

There was a line when we left, which means that you will have to wait a bit for your ice cream. It seems like everyone knew the wait time, once you go once it is easy to understand that it will take at least five minutes per person to get their ice cream. This isn't a scoop and go. But the wait is so worth it! Also, it wouldn't be customizable if they didn't take the time to make it! 

Also, even though when we got there, there was only one employee, it seems like they know their busy times because when we left there were three. 

I already want to go back, that's how much I enjoyed this place. They really exceeded my expectations, because I was going in with low spirits. If anyone wants to try this place, let me know, because I will happily join you.

Not the cleanest store and I didn't find what I was looking for.

I was looking for a cute box and nice colored pens. Basically, I was throwing a friend a sprinkle and I wanted people to be able to write nice messages to her and put them in a box. I could have gone to Target, but I'd never been to this Dollar Tree so I thought I'd test it out.

The main reason I didn't like it is it is pretty dirty. I have a one year old who goes straight for the garbage on the ground and he found a ton! Little beads, wrappers, flowers - you name it, he found it. 

Maybe I just don't know what people normally shop for at a Dollar Tree, but I couldn't find a cute box and I couldn't find colored pens. I found colored markers, but they were the cheap, thick kind. 

We walked up and down the aisles a bit and everything was pretty cheap looking, which I'm guessing is because it is all cheap. They do have quite a few party supplies so if you are throwing a birthday party/baby shower/etc. and want to save a few bucks, you should take a look!

We ended up leaving empty handed. I probably won't be back.

It neighborhood support ice cream joint. Just across the library. This ice cream place established since 1958. Very old store. You can tell they are very successful with many come to visit. It is a dairy joy to people here like it's name. 

I got the vanilla dip cone with butterscotch. Only few ice cream place has dip cone. This is one of them. The banana split here look very good. One day I will try it. The small cone we have is good quantity of the soft serve. They have good technique to swirl it up higher. Keep up the good work which attracts people here.

Ugh. I really have tried to give them the benefit of the doubt - mainly because they are so conveniently located near my office, but almost every visit since my 2016 review has been terrible.

I once had a technician who was so busy looking around at other customers she only remembered to try and get the gel off of three fingers instead of all five. Repeatedly. Until finally I said I was going to leave after having the gel removed unless they could provide someone else, which they finally did. 

My most recent visit, the technician asked me THREE separate times if I wanted a manicure in addition to my pedicure. This is after me actually saying "I only want a pedicure".  Also - her massage consisted of gently rubbing lotion on my legs and feet, with no pressure or actual massage included. I get that I did not price up to one of the overpriced specialty massages, but still. I also had to put my own sandals carefully on afterwards, because my technician couldn't be bothered.

The final straw is that they raised their prices, so for my sub-par pedicure, I paid $28 plus tip. Not even remotely worth it, and even for convenience sake I will not be back.
You can find varieties of Middle Eastern food here. There's one aisle dedicated for this. Also varieties of sweets and boxes of dates during Ramadan.

UBER EATS REVIEW:

i had such a craving for mapo tofu on a cold night i decided to place a delivery here since i had a good dine in review! i was so disappointed...

the mapo tofu was like an orange color, nowhere near the vibrant red and spice that i wanted. the tofu wasnt't even the soft kind that's usually used, it was hard and not what i wanted at all. no flavor, didn't taste good. i was so disappointed. 

spicy schezuan chicken was also disappointing. the chicken pieces were so tiny and not crispy at all. i put them in my air fryer hoping to get it crispy which it did but it also shrunk down the size of them even more

shrimp crispy pan noodles was OK. needed more flavor and seasoning.

all in all, this was such a disappointing order i wanted a refund so badly but nothing was missing but deliciousness.

Ordered delivery from Grubhub. I called the restaurant for some questions and the staff was friendly and knowledgeable about the menu.

I had Texas chopped salad. Very good and fresh with chopped apple and crispy deep-fried brussel sprouts. The dressing is a perfect compliment to the salad.

We were in the mood to do a puzzle so we called Amazing Discoveries. They gave us a brief rundown of what they had in stock. We asked them their COVID protocol and headed over. They don't let more than 6 people in the store at one time. Parking and entry is in the back due to roadwork and construction on Broadway. They have a table with sanitizer in the entry hallway as well. The staff kindly directed us to the puzzle shelf when we arrive. The store is super clean and spread out. Staff are friendly. We were in and out in less than ten minutes. We'd definitely come back again! They do have online ordering but it does not appear that the in-store inventory matches what they have online, so if you're looking for something specific, call first!

3/24/20 Update 

BEST WINGS IN TUCSON!!!! 

I'm so sad he's leaving for Tennessee at the end of March. (Take care if his dad.) My favorite part is the wingette because they are moist. But his drummettes are just as moist!!! And the nice light crisp of the skin to get into the moist, succulent meat is SO delicious! You can get a variety of styles but I just like his dry rub seasoning. Get 'em before he leaves!

The Pork U sandwich is SO good. His pulled pork is some of the best in Tucson. The Pablo Diablo, oh my. You may want to split a sandwich because they are so hearty. His fries are excellent.

This is a good place for drinks and dessert, but it's not great for its other food or for conversation.

The C House is another trendy offering up in Seminole Heights. Large, open air bar with live music, indoor is full of exposed beams, hard surfaces. The servers are very cool looking if not the most professional. They serve drinks in funky glassware. They definitely do not have enough parking in their lot.

Food was not great. We started with the charcuterie. We had the aged cheddar, the Manchego, the Serrano ham, the prosciutto, and the chorizo verde. The cheese and chorizo were great. I liked the presentation of the platter, and I thought the grapes and pickled green beans were very tasty. But the Serrano and the prosciutto were very disappointing. They were cut way too thick, and that changes the texture and makes them too salty. You really want the thinnest slices of those meats for the best experience.

We shared a caesar, and it was fine. I liked the cheese toast instead of croutons.

I had the Chicken Euphoria for my main. It was not euphoric. In the bun with the cole slaw and pickles, I couldn't even taste the chicken at all, so I ended up eating it plain. It was fine, but nothing special. I swapped out the fries for the charred avocado and wish I stayed with the fries. I don't know  if my avocado wasn't ripe or if the charring did something to the texture, but it just wasn't good. The avocado was mealy and flavorless.

My husband had the cod and chips, and he liked it.

The champagne list is impressive, but if you want nonsparkling wine, I found fewer good options. So we stuck to the cocktails. They were good if a little light on the booze.

We ate too much and thus took our dessert to go. My husband was underwhelmed by his chocolate chip cookie. I had the caramel cheesecake, and it was very rich and not too sweet. So I can happily recommend that C on their menu.

Not sure if I'd return. It was too loud for a good conversation. And the food wasn't great.

A year requires an update! So I've still yet to try their pizzas, but their hoagies and sandwiches have held up to the original standard. The service is always prompt, efficient and friendly. 

I ordered a new item a handful of times this year and it's their turkey burger. I usually get the cheeseburger lunch special which is served with fries, side salad, or bag of chips and can or fountain soda for roughly $8ish with tax and they've always allowed me to exchange for a turkey burger patty. It's a solid burger I always enjoy it. 

2020 I will try their pizza and update. Definitely my favorite casual neighborhood  pizza joint in East Falls

During pandemic, you can check in to the store online and get in line to wait. It is easy to check in. Usually the given time in the web is 10 minutes more than the actual wait time. Unless you did not show up on time and they give you spot to the next person. You just need to wait for the next turn. 

I have my hair cut without sideburn. The hair dresser did a good job for the to keep my hair short and not spiky. Good technique especially for the oriental hair. I got my cut within 15 minutes. It is definitely good to have it finish quick. Payment is easy. The hair dresser do have mask to cover.

What a delightful breakfast! So pleasant. Breakfast was delicious. Here's a tip if you are going on a holiday or on the weekend: Get there before 9:00 or you will be waiting in line for a table. Just wake up a little earlier. Of course, here on Yelp, it shows the typical busy times and the wait times. A pretty cool feature of the app.

My wife and I came here for Veteran's Day. Beautiful day, so we opted to eat outside on the patio. I'm impressed by how much space there is compared to their last location. Must be three times the seating. And there still was no parking in their lot, on the street, and the neighboring businesses' lots were filling up. Good thing those businesses are closed on the weekends.

My wife had the "All Hail the Omelette" and I had the Monte Cristo. There were way too many scrumptious choices on the menu and it was difficult narrowing it down. Guess I'll have to visit again and again.

My wife really enjoyed the omelette. She said that the flavors did not overwhelm each other and were nicely balanced. She also ordered a fresh fruit cup which came with pineapple, strawberries, and blueberries. Surprised it cost only a buck. The strawberries (they were also a garnish on my Monte Cristo) were the first strawberries we've had since leaving SoCal that actually tasted like strawberries and were sweet.

My Monte Cristo was satisfying. It is supposed to be made with a pineapple marscapone, but I didn't taste the pineapple that much. But it was still a good balance of flavors, nothing overwhelming each other. It comes with a fresh strawberry preserve, but I also asked for honey.

I ordered two eggs, over medium. This is the second time I've asked for it here, but they failed. Got it over easy again. Over medium is cooked longer than over easy, but not as long as over hard. The yolk should be slightly set, but creamy and not runny. 

I also had a stuffed brioche donut. Excellent. The texture was perfect. Yeasty enough to be light and fluffy and not thick. The filling was a perfect custard with a fruit flavoring. 

Great food, excellent service, nice ambiance. 

5* = A top favorite. Mind-blowing good. I will definitely return and I will go out of my way to go here. Worth every dime of my money.

4* = Outstanding. Tucson-top-tier. Some elements not quite as good as it gets, but it still is worthy of my regular business.

3* = Good experience. I might do repeat business if it is convenient, the only option, the only one in its category, or on a whim, but would not be among the first thing to come to mind. Would definitely recommend you trying it to get your own take on it.

2* = Not so good. Would not recommend at all. Waste of money.

1* = How are they still in business? Stay away at all costs!

They have a traveling froyo truck that will come to your neighborhood during COVID-19!

My neighbors are doing all the work for me and arranging for food trucks to come to our neighborhood. All I have to do it put it on my calendar and show up. These are the best type of neighbors. 

The froyo truck parked in my neighbor's driveway and was there between 4 p.m. an 7 p.m. on a Wednesday. We could come any time during those hours. Froyo for dinner? Yes, please!

The workers wore masks and there were cones on the ground to mark six feet apart. Still, with a window of three hours, we didn't have to wait in line. We saw neighbors coming and going, but we kept our distance. 

For $5 you get a cup of froyo and two toppings. For a dollar extra you could get homemade ice cream. Anything homemade captures my attention so I went for it. I got half peanut butter fudge and half salted caramel with butterfingers and reeses pieces for my toppings. Like I said, it was my dinner so I wanted all the calories! It did not disappoint.

The workers were so friendly and it was a nice treat during this time. If you have a group of friends looking for a business to support, call them up and have their truck come out!

Of the four restaurants I tried this year during restaurant week this was probably the most underwhelming. It's probably more happening when the attached hotel is fuller but when we went it was pretty dead as we were the only people there. 
First course was my favorite. I opted for the tomato soup with crab meat and it was very good. For my entree I got the jerk chicken. The jerk chicken I've had in the past had all had...jerk seasoning. This chicken was completely devoid of any seasoning. I don't understand. I will say the coconut rice with it was really good but the chicken was a disappointment. My boyfriend got the salmon which he really liked. I couldn't help but notice his salmon was very well seasoned but I'm not bitter or anything. 
Dessert and cocktails were both pretty good as well. And our waitress was really efficient and friendly. But sadly I can do a lot of things, but bland food isn't one of them, so I doubt I'll be back.

Solid Szechuan food with a solid tea smoked tea duck and traditional authentic dishes like dry woks and water boiled spicy fish. The chef is the same as the original Pin Wei so the food is still as solid as before.  A good option if you are local.

Largest of the Ollies I've been too. Product repeats in different sections of the store says there's too much space, not enough product for the location, but I suppose that means they can always get more stuff in. 

We went before Phase 3 opened, so they were still social distancing, they were still wearing masks (And should be still). Their book section was HUGE and well spaced out. They have lots of coloring books and reader glasses, if you're the type who doesn't want the stigma of always wearing specs. 

Toys were prevalent, we saw they had the teenage mutant ninja turtles, a whole set, but one of them was boxed wrong so it looked like you'd have gotten 2 Raphaels, but you would have actually gotten them all. A collectors gaffe. We didn't buy it. 

The store was disorganize but fun.

Love the atmosphere, love the cheese and charcuterie board, underwhelmed with my salad.

That's where that last star went, I wasn't a huge fan of my main dish, which was the Canadian Maple Salad with grilled shrimp. I thought I was going to love it, it has maple in the name, but I didn't really enjoy the dressing or feel like there was much else to make it stand out. The shrimp was delicious though.

That's the only major negative to my experience. If you decide to just stick with the cheese and charcuterie board, you will have a five star meal. The accoutrements are really where this dish shines. I loved everything pickled and the nuts roasted in house. I don't remember all the cheeses we got, but I know I was so so on the burrata (which surprised me, because I love burrata, but it wasn't as creamy as I'm used to), but I enjoyed the brie and the manchego. I wasn't able to try the meats since I don't eat pork.

The salted caramel cheesecake was to die for. I would order it every time. I love sweet and salty so it really hit home for me. 

Service was good, a little slow, but this is the type of place you relax with a good cocktail and great friends. 

The parking lot gets full quickly, but you can park in the neighborhood as long as you don't block a driveway!

Nice little watch repair place that can do everything from simple battery replacements to watch service to luxury repair. I tested them out by bringing my Movado for a battery repair, and I was very happy with the service I received. I'll happily return if one of my fancier watches needs attention.

I can't believe it's taken me this long to try this place. It's a dive bar that allows smoking (so not exactly my thing), but the food they serve is really good and cheap. I had the Reuben. It was well-toasted, full of corned beef and tangy sauerkraut. The potato salad it came with tasted homemade (good). The serving size was amazing for the price (only $7.95). Most importantly, the food was ready in 5 minutes.

I'll happily return for another sandwich.

A lot of varieties of cheese and different shapes of dry pasta, fresh mozzarella. Service is fast and efficient. If you want good quality of cured meats such as salami, prosciutto, you have to go to this place. They taste so much better than the ones at the grocery store.

Although they sell Italian bread, I recommend Sarcone's a block south for your perfect Italian hoagie.

Claudio is also a good place for your antipasti needs . They make marinated red peppers, sun dried tomatoes, varieties of olives, marinated mozzarella.

This place is super crowded during holidays

Hayman Jewelry was recommended to me by a friend when I mentioned to her that I had jewelry left to me by my Grandmother that I never wore because it was too heavy and / or too bulky.  They are a small store located in downtown Tampa, with a wide selection of merchandise that seemed to be available for every price point. You have to park on the street at an available meter, which was the only downside to their location. They are a family owned and operated business, open since 1933, and could not have been more friendly and genuine.

I brought my pieces in and brainstormed with Alison, one of the family members, and we came up with some great ideas. I never felt rushed to decide what I wanted, and Alison was tireless when it came to texting me different options available for my pieces.

I ended up having one pair of sapphire earrings turned into a pair of stud earrings for me, and hanging pendants that I gave to each of my nieces, one ring turned into a pendant, and one ring turned into a simpler ring. I love every new piece I received, and I know that I'll be turning to Hayman's in the future when I need new jewelry.

St. Mary's Mexican Food reminds of Nico's taco shop in a lot ways. The food, at least what we ordered, is on par with a fast food taco joint. It's fast and cheap, but not the greatest, in my opinion. I have not tried some of their signature items like the menudo and red chile. Out order was two tostadas, carne seca burro and a breakfast burrito (bacon, egg , potato, cheese). They were a-ok. The highlight of the breakfast burrito is that the potatoes are finely diced. I wish more places would do it that way! The line was a little long but moved fast. However, that's not always the case. You can call for pick up and that's highly recommended.

Poor quality of meat with no flavor and canned tasting beans. Hot dogs were flavorless, buns were soggy, veggies were old. Waitress repeated our order twice but kept forgetting to bring us all of our food. 

It wasn't busy this early evening so their should not have been any excuse for their slow service. 

Ample parking with drive thru. Prices too much for tasteless food and sub-par service. They have no kids menu either which is bizarre to me...

Authentic Cuisine #69 -  Guyanese Cuisine 

This grocery store has an kitchen who serve West Indian with Guyanese cuisine. It is interesting to see the curry is a touch different than most. I have been book mark this place for years, finally I was able to visit one of my 1700 bookmarks. I like grocery setup. But I like their bakery and the food they provided. 

Bakery 
I got the the pine tart. This tart is pineapple filling with good crust. I also got the the curry chicken and jerk chicken pastry. Both of them is good. Especially the the jerk chicken pastry. 

Food
We start with the Pholourie. This Guyana snack food is a fried dough with tamarind sauce. Very interesting. It is popular item and only available Saturday. We also got the Doubles.
