This is a street food that serve there. For me is ok. We got the chicken chow mein. The fried noodle is good but I prefer Chinese fried noodle. The chicken was marinated with good sauce. A bit dried. The last dish is the goat curry. I like this the best. It come with dhal puri which is Indian bread with split pea stuff inside. 

Our experience here is good. The price is reasonable. Love to try different item in the bakery and food in the future.

Decent fast food at the International Plaza food court. 

If you want a faster meal than you'd get at a fast-casual restaurant like Outback, you can go to Aussie Grill by Outback for many of the same food items, made easy to eat at a mall.

My husband got the volcano shrimp and a basic burger. I had the grilled chicken sandwich. My kids had the kids meals with crispy chicken.

Everything was pretty good, although a little salty (which, I know, is part of why they are good). My chicken had a good amount of spicy kick from the jalapenos, and the meat was juicy. My husband liked his burger, although he did not want bacon on it (weird, I know!). My kids liked their chicken and french fries.

The IP mall food court is usually pretty clean and is always well-lit and airy. And there are a lot of seats, many with power outlets. It is a convenient place to have a quick meal.

So many things were great about Xochitl. Ambiance is super cool and cozy, location can't be beat, drinks are on point. Everything was great except the lack of flavor in almost everything I tasted. 
The signature guacamole in theory was awesome. It features unique ingredients like pineapple, pomegranate seeds, etc. Yet managed to be completely void of flavor. My boyfriend thought it was hummus. I don't understand how this had no taste unless they don't use fresh ingredients. I don't understand. 
My favorite thing I tried was queso fundido with chorizo. That was on point and I loved how it was served with tortillas. 
For dinner I got a chicken tinga burrito which again had very little flavor. It was just completely uninspired. In my estimation mexican food should be super flavorful but this was not the case at all. 
We tried the churros for dessert and they were the only churros I've ever tried that didn't have cinnamon or sugar on them. Just greasy dough. Which isn't terrible but wasn't what I was expecting. 
I would come here again and just get drinks because their drinks were great. I sampled the blood orange margaritas and Mexican hot chocolate both of which were great. While the food here doesn't do it for me there's enough redeeming qualities to possibly bring me back for a drink.

This deli is order at the counter and issue an order number.  Then you a fix you number to table stand. That how they identify your order and deliver to you. Table and seat is pick by you. It has freedom of your table choice. I like that concept of fast food order. They bring the food to you. 

The menu is very simple. Mainly is the sandwich. Nothing fancy. We got the Black Angus Beef Club. The club sandwich come as four pieces of bread cut in triangle with beef slide sandwich. The bacon can be improved to a bit crispy. This item may be one of the most expensive items. Taste is ok. 

We waited about 8 minutes for our food to deliver to our table. Not much of service

They always have great deals and the customer service for me has always been excellent. I've done drive thru and walk in (pre-Corona). Soft serve cones (cups now because of Corona) for .50 at the mo. Combo deals. They gave away kids meals sans toys during the early Corona lockdown. The food is inexpensive and the quality is... Affordable. I have fun visiting and they always take care of me. I'm not a fast food regular but it's an occasional treat and keeps me up with how the other half live when I'm not doing Berns every night.

I had a great experience with 2 College Brothers on my recent move.  I contacted them multiple times leading up to my move and their estimates and information they provided were spot on.  Scheduling was also very easy given the complexity of my situation with closing on the house I was selling, closing on the house I was buying and trying to move from one place to the other all in a matter of 2 days.  

Chase and Josh G. showed up right on time and worked fiercely to get everything wrapped and loaded up in record speed.  I moved into a condo so it was no easy feet with having to street park the truck and unload it through a freight elevator to get it up to my unit.  Despite this they worked diligently and had everything done before the time estimate I was given.  My furniture and items all arrived in one piece and I couldn't have been more please with the efficiency, speed, and cost that this company provided.

I've been to this location a few times over the past few years and I noticed on my most recent visit that quality of food and the location, itself, has gone downhill.  Not to mention, there isn't much value for the quality/quantity of food you get compared to similar restaurants in the area. 

The restaurant felt dingy and our booth was a bit sticky and grimy.  Service was pretty average and the Grilled Chicken on the Barbie I had was actually flavored very well.  I did substitute the mixed vegetables for 4 sprigs of asparagus, which resulted in an upcharge for some reason.  Also, for an entrée that included a breast of chicken and four asparagus, I don't know that $20 seems like a good deal.

Wow! I love pita jungle. We just ordered food from pita jungle through postmates, the delivery came and there were missing items. I called and explained that a few items were missing and the assistant manager, Malik was so sweet and offered to drive the missing food over to our place. He did not have to do that and he insisted. It was extremely kind and I appreciate his positivity when he showed up! 
Not to mention, the chicken Schwarma was so incredible the hummus.... so good. Such a good Arizona favorite and great customer service is always a bonus.

My Vietnamese friend recommended this to me and  its my favorite place for lunch or dinner! The owners are very sweet, the food is always hot and delicious, the ingredients are fresh and clean and the restaurant is cozy and casual. I love the fried egg rolls, #20 rare steak pho, and the chicken curry! I'll have to try some more stuff but those are my staples! Def recommend this place
Love this place always a great variety of local and micro brews. Whether just looking for a place to chill or hang with friends it is a great spot

The best Vietnamese in Tampa, very authentic! We moved to Riverview area and really miss this Pho! Whenever we are in the area we make it a point to go. There broth is so flavorful, always fresh and a big serving of herbs. I always get the hot jasmine tea, they bring a full pot! My husband loves their lemonade, it's made in-house. You will not be disappointed!!
Hands down the best ceviche in Tampa. Incredible dishes, regardless of what you order. Yes it's a little pricey but worth every bite.

This isn't your typical grocery store. This is the store you run to when you get a call from your husband calls you and says that his boss is coming over for dinner! It's full of great things that would be hard to find elsewhere. 

We stopped in to get something to take out for lunch. The choices are huge, deli counter, sushi, salad bar, sandwiches, pizza, and on & on. They also have a great wine and beer selection.

It's all good!

So we've gotten pizza from here a few times at work. They don't skimp on toppings, which I appreciated. So in terms of my personal preferences for pizza, I'm not really a doughy person. If I'm getting thin crust, I like the crust to be light and crispy. This crust was more of a doughy and chewy. The pie bottom was also a bit more on the doughy side. I personally like it a little thinner. Cheese was plentiful, and it came piping hot so all positive notes. I would say if it was available, I would eat it. I don't think I personally would go out of my way to buy it myself though.

Good service. Good food. High energy. Large portions. Low prices. I could not ask for more than this. Left work and came here for breakfast on a Friday morning, and the parking lot was surprisingly full of cars. Despite the crowd, my coworker and I were seated immediately. From the time we were seated to the time we received our meals, about 7 mins had elapsed. These people are pros who are prepped to deal with the pressure of a large crowd in a timely manner. My friend & I both got the special (2 eggs, 2 bacon, 2 sausage, 2 pancakes-$5.75) and were astonished by the size of the plates. Neither she nor I was able to finish the meal, & had to get the leftovers to go. Our waitress was full of jokes, and seemed to genuinely enjoy her job and coworkers. If you're reading this and have never eaten here, give it a shot, it's totally worth

This place was aight. I got spaghetti & clams which were ok, kind of watery and not the most flavorful. The menu was also pretty basic. But, the service was great, food came out fast, and the restaurant had a nice homey feel.

Just moved to this area....sleep clock is all messed up.  Woke up at 5:00 AM and not able to make breakfast as we are still unpacking.  Found this place on Yelp and decided to give a try...not many other places open at this time of the morning.  Glad we did! Service was excellent and breakfast was too.

I cannot believe my experience at R2L. I walked in with a ton dead bugs outside. I can understand this is out of the restaurant and buildings control. But when I walked through the rotating doors a large insect flew straight into my eye. I let it go because I understand this is probably still most likely out of the restaurants control. We were greeted nicely and directed where to go upstairs. When we got there at 8 for my reservation time, the hostess said a table was not yet ready and we could take a seat at the bar. It was not a big deal at first, but we were there for almost 15 minutes, when a fire alarm started blaring. The bartenders continued on with their jobs, which I found somewhat professional, but when I asked what the problem was, they nonchalantly said "I don't know" as fire alarms blasted in our ears. I asked the hostess if we could leave and I got an aggressive answer of "The elevator is not in service but we could take the stairs but then fire alarm will me done before we got down, and it should be done in 10 or less minutes". I would have walked down if my fiancée was not in heels. We waited for another 45 minutes at the bar with lights flashing with no easy access to get out. We sat at the bar and got drinks as alarms kept ringing and felt trapped. Over an hour later of our reservation time, we were seated with no mention of the events that just took place. The appetizers were good but my steak I ordered medium rare was grey throughout most of it. My fiancée got the "restaurant week" deal that included a sorbet with unruled fruit. I really can't believe that this nice of an establishment would be so passive to alarms blaring for almost an hour without working elevators to their customers. My ears are still ringing.

The view was good (and similar to one I had just seen from a hospital room), the food was good (restaurant week portions) but be careful with the service....the reservation requires a deposit which is kinda unusual but the worse part was our waiter decided to double his tip.  We had a gift card and told him to give himself a $40 tip but he decided to process it as $80 and then we were given the balance on our gift card.  Had the balance on our GC not be checked by me, he would have made $80. Luckily we caught it and now have a major sour taste for this place.  Someone fixed it for us but the waiter never can back to apologize...his tail must have been caught between his legs.  Buyer beware. Should have lowered his tip but I'm not like that.  Kinda wish I ddI tho.

We stopped by on a whim because we wanted to try a nice restuarant with a great view in Philly. And boy...were we pleasantly surprised! I rarely give 5 stars so this is legit an awesome place.

We didn't make reservations in advance, but we were allowed to sit at the bar or lounge area. It was a surprisingly nice spot because the lounge area had a great view. You get the full dinner menu at the bar/lounge too. 

It comes with a free bread basket with butter. The bread was ok....a bit too hard. We ordered the 20 oz ribeye ($48) made medium rare which was the best steak I've ever had in my life. It was crispy on the outside but the tenderest and juiciest meat on the inside. The lobster mac and cheese ($20) was good with a lot of lobster pieces but it was placed on a narrow boat dish. Which made it difficult to get the mac and cheese off the plate. I would pass on the mac and cheese next time and stick with the ribeye. The warm vanilla cake ($12) was delicious! The cake had a center filling which looked like custard but not as sweet. It had a side of vanilla ice cream. 

This place was phenomenal! Perfect for date night or a special occasion. Next time, I'm going to make reservations for a window seat.
We went on Easter Sunday. We had drinks at the bar before dinner. The food was very good and the service was also attentive.

As a yelper I'm embarrassed that I didn't take any pictures of the food this week.  The chips and salsa and guacamole were awesome. Between the four of us we had a carnitas burrito, carmelo with carne asada and all but one of the taco meats.  The food was so good and we were so hungry that we didn't think of taking pictures.  So if you're thinking of trying it's definitely a thumbs up from us.

Second visit I actually took pictures! The queso fundido chorizo was my absolute favorite! Don't pass this place up!

The view was good (and similar to one I had just seen from a hospital room), the food was good (restaurant week portions) but be careful with the service....the reservation requires a deposit which is kinda unusual but the worse part was our waiter decided to double his tip.  We had a gift card and told him to give himself a $40 tip but he decided to process it as $80 and then we were given the balance on our gift card.  Had the balance on our GC not be checked by me, he would have made $80. Luckily we caught it and now have a major sour taste for this place.  Someone fixed it for us but the waiter never can back to apologize sohis tail must have been caught between his legs.  Buyer beware.

I don't know why I keep thinking I'm going to get a good meal.  I keep trying again and again.  Each time it seems worse and worse.  I remember the old times when this place was the sh**, but those days appear to be over.  Today I was ravenous and was served tacos that were so soggy that I had to eat them with a knife and fork.  They serve them on a thin piece of paper, so the only way to eat the tacos was to also eat the paper.  I was so hungry I didn't have another choice.  Never again.  I'm done.
Love everything about Independent. The food and beer are amazing. The pork tacos, Cuban sandwich, and caprese panini are our favorites and try the side salad!
This place has not only never let me down, but it has amazed me everytime I eat there.  Try the Jalepeno Cilantro Hummus...  It is tge beat Hummus I've ever had.  Hands down!

This place has good food, especially the beans, but the line can take up to 1/2 an hour just to ORDER!  Them it's another half an hour to get your food.  This place is an all-day affair. If you call in, it's marginally faster--IF you can get through to them.  They need to staff more people. Maybe 5 more people. One to answer the phones and get drinks, two for orders, one to bus tables and clean and another cook/prep.  They are usually out of tortillas by the time I get to the front of the line.

I understand why the stars might be lower as the menu is limited and this is not the place I would pick for authentic Mexican food. 

Came here for a last minute reservation on a Saturday night and when we arrived before our table was ready the hostess let us order togo cocktails to walk around with a sip. The yucca fries were delicious and the platanos fritos were good but are unexpectedly sweet not savory! Would order again but as more of a desert option

This restaurant is a long time gem in Tucson. For authentic French cuisine it is always impeccably "spot on" to truly French style classics. The menu is experienced in their presentations which has been truly excellent throughout the years. I absolutely enjoy dining here as that it is an experience being lost in the Tucson area. Plan on staying for two or more hours to enjoy a delicious lunch or dinner. Bon appétit!

Authentic decor, and such but the menu is definitely not good. I did not enjoy the food at all. I would say only the salad and the lamb was ok the rest was pretty mediocre. There are way better restaurants in the area where you could get much better food for a similar price. Wouldn't go back there again.

We stopped in for a beer but saw they had a Northern Liberties cheesesteak.  We got to Northern Liberties cheesesteak.  Spoiler alert: it is a roast beef panini.  It is a good roast beef panini.  Nice place.  Very helpful people.  Huge Beer selection.  $7.  There are a lot of reasons to love the Foodery in Northern Liberties.

I've lived away from South Philly for about 15 years and was home for a visit. I was staying about a block away and my extended family drove in from SJ to meet for dinner, so Ralph's was a great option.

They arrived and valet parking ($10.00) is absolutely the best way to go.
The guy was fast and friendly and street parking can be a pain.

I had made a reservation on Open Table and they had a table ready when we arrived.

Our server came by to get drinks, the busboy​ brought a basket of bread. The bread is what south Philly is known for, crunchy outside, slightly chewy but light and fluffy inside. 

The server seemed a little annoyed when my brother-in-law asked for a glass for his beer and we only had one appetizer,​ but it's also part of the south Philly charm.

The place is old school, iconic and the oldest Italian restaurant​ in the county. It's an old three-story​ "Trinity" {livingroom, dining room​, kitchen, straight shot, front to back}. It's got great old tiles, historic pictures and you sit right on top of the next table.

Not much has changed in the last century, which is why it is so amazing.

My brother-in-law ordered a Meatball and Ricotta Cheese appetizer. it was two good-sized, hand-rolled​, perfectly​ seasoned meatball in homemade​ gravy with a dollop of whipped ricotta. {do not share this, get your own and use a lot of bread to wipe up the gravy}.

I had narrowed my choices to about six things, {you just don't great Italian food in Florida}. 

Two people at the table ordered Chicken Parmesan, Veal Parmesan​ and split them and shared. The veal was pounded thin, lightly breaded, tender and covered the large oval plate. The chicken was two thicker piece cutlets, both were perfectly covered with cheese and gravy.

The other dishes, Veal Rollatine , ​and a penne dish got rave​ reviews.

I decided on the Lasagna, I was served a nice size portion, perfectly layered and firm where the ricotta cheese and gravy stole the show and the noodles and ground meat were there to hold it up. It was pretty delicious.

It's not fancy, it's home, it's​ not sauce, its GRAVY, it's homemade, its history, it's South Philly.

St Mary's mexican food easily makes my list of top 5 best mexican restaurants in Tucson! The chips are cooked to perfection and guac is creamy. I could eat these tacos every day.. the birria was full of flavor! Only downfall is they came in crispy shells, which I didn't know, so transferred them to soft shell when I got home. All items are super affordable and staff were friendly when I picked up my call ahead order. I will absolutely be back!

They have some tasty sangria and a few good vegan options. Somewhat pricy but what you would expect for the area. I had the vegan and Sara bowl which was pretty good. They have an event space and do various events and I'm excited to go back to try more and maybe hit up emo night. I wish they had a few more vegan options but I can't be too picky!

I used to love this restaurant but it has steadily gone down hill. Poor service, foods not good anymore, and it's dead. I've enjoyed many great meals here but I'm sad to say I will not be returning to over pay for mediocre food. Management reach out to me to address these concerns.

Bella Prana is clean and welcoming. Parking is across the street, behind the building, and on the street and I have never had a problem finding a spot. 

What brings me to Bella is not only the warm (lighting and fish tank adds a nice touch) environment, but an instructor, Lisa.  Lisa Wilson is, hands down, the best prenatal, baby, toddler, family yoga instructor in Tampa Bay. I live in St Petersburg, but drive to South Tampa because my 2 yo son LOVES Lisa and her class. She makes yoga fun for the kids and has a lot of singing and movement (aka bonding time with your little ones). Lisa is also a fabulous prenatal  yoga instructor and creates a nice, relaxing, and supportive environment for all the hormonally-driven pregnant women who just need some "me time"...with backaches, cankles, and all!

Tiny place that looked like it had potential. We dined in but had to wait nearly an hour for our food because priority was given to all the to-go orders. Food wasn't bad but it wasn't really great either...and certainly not worth waiting over a half hour for. I've actually lost my faith in yelp reviews after going to this place.
The rooster salad was on point. Great service from Bailley too. Will definitely return to try more things.

Have always passed by this place but glad I finally  came in. They pretty much have everything here, coffee, drinks, lunch, brunch, music. Staff was very friendly, and very helpful about what to order. Place is cool and clean, with clean bathrooms...rare in Ybor. 

Drinks were spot on, and presented well. Even the coffee drink with dessert was just right. Great prices too if you're here for just some drinks. Bar area seating, indoor seating, and an outdoor back patio. 

This queso is the best I've had. Even compared to the Mexican restaurants I've been too. Tacos were assembled and put together just right, seasoned and tasting just right. Pork sandwich assembled and presented well. Plate cleaned. To finish it all off, strawberry shortcake. Light, airy, not too sweet. 

Hope to be back soon. I really want to even pick up some queso to bring home.

Awesome wings great salad and of course their pizza is the best ever. 
Service is very attentive for the lunch rush. Nothing crazy I do like that this store has more windows they allow light into. Several stores recently like in carrollwood and Brandon like it darker not sure why

One of the few things I miss about Tucson is St. Mary's Mexican Food..The green corn tamales are amazing the best I ever eaten The prices are very reasonable in price and most of all delicious. Very friendly family oriented.  I can remember them from many decades back.

If I lived in Philly this would definitely be the perfect pre game spot. It's chill, affordable and they have unlimited drinks to choose from. I was pretty basic in my food choice. I got the grilled cheese and fries on the side. I mean there is no way they could mess it up, but MAN it was good! I also had so many cider options that I choose from, their choices aren't only limited to beer. I wish they had one of these in NYC.
Good quality Bread good quality meat and good quality service, the staff is very friendly and the service was good and fast. I had the italian hoagie it was very good. I will be returning
The service at this restaurant has really deteriorated. Given my most recent experience, I would not recommend showing up for their "happy hour"-- very disappointing.

Good place to enjoy comfort food and a beverage. I splurged and enjoyed a cup of chili, blue cheese wedge salad along with sweet ginger wings. The wedge salad was terrific. Probably should of orders 2 instead of the wings but all was good. I left comfortably happy and stuffed.
Hit or miss. Well thought out flavors, staff is friendly. Some times the donuts are incredible, other times they are not.

I was frequenting this place a lot lately on account of my baby's many food intolerances. This restaurant has a great vegan and allergen menu which was awesome for me to be able to actually go out and eat without worrying. My review would initially have been five stars on account of good food and decent service. However during my last visit I was disgusted, when I saw one of the cooks exiting the bathroom while zipping up his fly. To me it looks like he did his business, didn't wash his hands, and proceeded to walk straight back into the kitchen (which is visible to the public) and continue cooking. So gross. We were on our way out when I saw this happen otherwise I wouldn't have eaten the food. Won't be going back to this location.

Update: I was in contact with the owner who is very kind. Per Kevin and the manager at this location, the hand hygiene issue was addressed with the cooks. I wasn't asked any specifics of when this happened or who it was but I'm hoping this helps prevent it from happening again.

Dry and overcooked chicken.  When they overload it with veggies, you get what you pay for.  Wouldn't have gone here anyway due to bad experience in the past, but I was overruled.  Oh well. Way overpriced for what you get.  Even more annoyed about the price.

Came without a reservation. I live in the area so I know better. It was my niece's 11th birthday and I knew she would appreciate the view. Although they were fully committed, the layout has the bar and the cocktail lounge with seating on either side of the bar in the front end of the dining room. So even if you can't grab a table you won't feel like an outcast. The bar and both sections of the cocktail lounge are adjacent to the dining room. I had the clam chowder and a chop salad. Both were unique and different than what I expected but delightfully so, and extremely delicious. My niece had the nachos which  also wrre different than what she was assuming but very delicious. We both had fillets. Cooked to perfection at medium temperature. I skipped dessert to slurp down a third bloody Mary, while my niece enjoyed the warm vanilla cake and a decaf cappuccino. Everyone was very pleasant and full of smiles. Sarah who  took care of us was knowledgeable of the entire menu and extremely pleasant as well as overwhelmingly beautiful. Her & my niece hit it right off. I greatly appreciated her going the extra mile with my niece.  All said and done I'm glad we wound up in the cocktail lounge, in the very capable hands of Sarah and we really enjoyed this night. Thank you guys.

Phillies games are obviously fantastic...but this review is the Questlove Cheesesteak. Get it. It will change your life, regardless of whether or not you're a vegetarian. I think about it every day. Thanks Questlove.

The service was really bad and the food was not good at all I'm very disappointed. They served me raw shrimp
And the Mgr didn't really address it. They were busy but not that busy at all. Don't come here.

We were travelling through the area and had stopped in Tucson for a couple of nights to recoup before heading off further through the country, and so that we could take in a couple of the sites in Tucson. 

It was time for dinner, and we really wanted something more authentic and local, rather than a chain restaurant that had all the things that we could get at home. While we have taquerias back in Florida, we aren't as close to Mexico as Tucson is, and there is a strong Cuban influence on the cuisine as well, so finding a local place seemed ideal.

We were not disappointed with this place! Pulling in, we saw there were a couple of Sheriff's office cars parked out front, which is always a good sign. Law enforcement officers know where the good places in town to eat are! 

Once inside, the smell of good Mexican food hit us like a brick, and we perused the menu with glee. All kinds of things leaped out at us, with various menu items that we had simply not seen before. Regional differences happen, even with taquerias.

My mother and I both ordered some tacos and burros, and together ordered a quesadilla to share. The tacos and burros were very good, and we had different meats so we were able to taste each of them. However, the star of the show was the quesadilla, which my mother described as "the best I have ever had". This says a lot, because she has been all over the world a number of times, and tasted a lot of different things in her life.

Hats off to Taqueira Pico de Gallo in Tucson... the next time we are in town, we will certainly search you out for a reprisal of the food you serve!
Finding anywhere that delivers breakfast is always a challenge. To find a AMAZING BREAKFAST SPOT, that delivers is a unicorn! My go-to Sunday mornings

I want to love this place. It's adorably located on head house square, sandwiched between Bloomsday and Malbec. Also, not many Mexican restaurants nearby to compete with. 

So why is this only 3 stars? The food is okay. The drinks are okay. The service is okay. That's what gets you 3 stars. It's edible and doesn't appear unclean. 

Advice: capitalize on the new crowds heading this way for Bloomsday. Hire friendlier, more charismatic bartenders. Do you want to be a bar that has TVs? Then be a bar that has TVs. This means playing sports, listening to customers that want certain things played, have game day specials, etc. If you don't want to be a bar that has TVs, then get rid of them. Finally, can't stress this one enough - get on Instagram! And post daily about your TVs, new bartenders, and amazing food, drink specials and events.
Great pizza and wings.  Good vibe and there is outdoor eating.  Enjoy with a date or family

Cafe-style restaurant in Ybor with trendy atmosphere. Wait staff was attentive and gave good menu suggestions. Drink menu wasn't too impressive but the food was great. Dress is casual. Parking is either street or the lost behind the stores. I'd suggest this place for lunch or an early dinner.
2nd time they run out of vodka in a week, how does a bar restaurant stay I business.. I won't be back..this place is not professional. Terrible

I was the co-host of a Meetup group last nite on the patio.  A shout out to Sabrina, our waitress. She was polite & proficient @her job!. The kitchen seemed to be overwhelmed & it took a loong time for our food to arrive, but it was tasty as usual.  I had the delicious seared Ahi tuna w/ 2 different types of house made hummus. I spoke to Malik, the mgr on duty, & he was very apologetic for the long wait.


Attn:  Kevin B., Customer Service, 
Thanks for Ur concern about our (20 diners, 2 tables) group waiting so long for our food to arrive,. I was unable to access service@pitajungle.com. U may email me @bilkanichj@yahoo.com or phone me (I'll email my phone # to U).  -Many thanks, Jeff B.

Beautiful. Beautiful. Food. If you're a foodie who values quality and a flavor profile that is one-of-kind...this is for you. This food is not frozen or processed--only the freshest ingredients with no cut corners. There is no menu and excellence is the standard. For the Octopus Falafel man, this work is his passion and it shows so clearly in his food. The charcoal grill is key but also his marinades and ingredients are unique and pulled together in a way that is both creative, fun, and down right spectacularly delicious. He is an artist. As one passer by explained "His cart if the best perfume in the world." If there was a 6-Star option for Yelp I'd have chosen it. His food is a masterpiece and I feel lucky to have had it.
We had a sampling of everything. I don't think there was one dish I did not love. Everything was flavorful and fresh.

Was able to make reservations for a Friday date night. We were seated upon arrival. There is live music which definitely set the mood with the intimate lighting. Lots of menu options and most of them sounded delicious, I could hardly make up my mind. We ordered drinks and food which both came out promptly. Server was friendly and offered an alternative whiskey for my date when his preferred wasn't available. I ordered the tostones con carne- unfortunately, the tostones got quite soggy throughout my meal sitting in the juice. My date got the chaufa de carne which is kind of like a fried rice dish. I liked his meal both visually and taste wise. Overall, I would definitely return for another date night.

I wanted to check out this grocery store and very glad I did. I was able to find some authentic Greek cheeses I had been missing from back home. In Chicago these cheeses are so easy to get and have so many different varieties than this brand. But this brand will do for the time being. Overpriced yes, but was able to purchase real feta made with sheep and goats milk, kasseri and mzyithra. This particular brand of Mt Vikos is PDO which I can appreciate knowing the origin and the authenticity. Mt Vikos brand for feta and kasseri I knew. But the mzyithra is not labeled to know who makes it says it comes from Mesa AZ. Also this piece of mizithra was made with pasteurized sheeps milk but should be raw plus I wanted the whole piece the entire round circle of it not just a chunk. The lady at cheese counter said the one who does the order was not at store so maybe next time I can find out.  

The store has a large variety of things. But I won't lie you can get alot of the same things alot lower priced elsewhere. The hard to find items not so much so it is definitely worth coming here to browse. They have an outdoor eating area for guests and misters on the lanai a very nice touch. 

Staff I came across was friendly and helpful. I was curious about the bakery and asked if items were made inhouse from scratch was told no..what a bummer. Store is clean and dim lit almost in a very odd spa/therapeutic way in which I liked and never noticed at any other grocer I been to except the ones saving on electricity..that have a 5 gallon bucket trying to catch rain leaks from the roof.  In which this was not the case here it was definitely an added touch to the shopping experience in a pleasant way.

The best food truck in the city!!!! The line can be long sometimes but it's soooo worth it!! The man cooks with his heart and passion! And or $10, you get chicken, rice, 3 or 4 kinds of veggies and a few kinds of fruits too. If you haven't tried it, you have to!! I'd give it 10 stars if I could.

This is an example of a place making it on location rather then food.  We went because we got a gift card.  My old fashion was ok, the risotto was ok, sliders were well done.  Wife liked her lobster and Mac and cheese.  Will go again because we still have money on the gift card.  I wouldn't spend my own cash here though.
Cuban sandwich poorly pressed lacking ingredients and just disgusting. The milk shake was nasty as well, churros are mini. Disappointed spent 50$ on trash. Go somewhere else trust me

This place is fantastic.  It's such a nice experience, from the hand-washing with rose water, to the endless courses.  The ambience transports you outside of Philly and into a calming atmosphere.  For the quantity and quality of food, it is also quite reasonably priced.  The only thing that would improve it for me is if the pita bread had better texture.  It's a bit tough and dry for my taste.  But really everything is great here.
Love this place. Lori and Mila are magical for the mani pedi! The only place to go in phily ! Thank you!

AJ's Fine Foods is a grocery store you'll want to enjoy. I am the quintessential run in and out grocery shopper and even I find myself pursuing the aisles surprised and delighted by the items here. I think they shine in the bakery and prepared foods. Whenever I'm in a potluck pinch the prepared food here gets me out of it! 

They also have a terrific coffee with the loyalty program as well. You can enjoy your coffee and perhaps a baked good on their windowed patio so you can enjoy the sights without sweating it out. 

I tend to go to their patio to meet friends and we can all grab a bite or a drink of our choice without anyone having to concede to eat/drink something they don't want. You want coffee? No prob! Your friend wants sushi? Have at it! Want to grab a quinoa salad? They got that too!

The Independent comprises a great laid-spot for beers for the afternoon and evening, especially on a late Sunday morning and afternoon if you want some chill vibes. 

The latter point is what drew us to The Independent, where I could knock back a couple of beers, including noshing on an egg & cheese croissant sandwich with bacon that was quite satisfying and tantalizing on the senses. 

Originally a gas station per the structural exterior, The Independent works very well as a Biergarten, with its cedar wood bar and walls on the inside and chill outdoor seating.

They have a pervasive selection of craft beer on tap and in a bottle and can. Many of the beers come from Tampa-St. Pete breweries, but others come from all over the country and the globe. 

The highlight beer I had was the Oude Geuze Boon by Brouwerij Boon that was a delicious lambic that definitely had lovely citrus and sour palate. Between the beer and the breakfast, I was pleased with my experience at The Independent. And the beertenders were chill and affable, which was a plus as we hung out at the bar on a Sunday late morning.

This is a great place to do a meeting, the service is relaxed and moves at your pace.  They are not pestering you every 5 minutes interrupting a discussion.  Sitting outside in the unrushed atmosphere is unique!  I've tried the Cuban, the hummus, and lots of different beers and they were all great!

I was stationed in Italy my first 3 years in the military and have been struggling to find amazing pizza ever since... I was very spoiled! I found an amazing place when I was stationed in Texas and was very happy to stumble upon this place while here in Tampa on business! My team and I ordered the white pizza with prosciutto and the carnivore, as well... and they were both absolutely delicious!!! This is absolutely at the top of my list for the next time I'm in town and would definitely recommend this place to pizza lovers!!!
There tuna hoagie are the best there fresh and for filling the bread is the best not to too soft or too hard sorry there no pic
Such a cute Italian restaurant. It was a little cramped, but the food was delicious! Went on a weekday for lunch and was surprised at the price (cheaper than expected) for the amount of food you get.

Great place for live music, local and traveling bands, and cold drinks. Bartenders know a lot of different drinks and the variety of liquor and drafts is wide. I think live music starts at like 9. Small door cover charge at night but worth it. Ladies get in free some nights.

I was driving through the south west so OF COURSE I needed a good taco.  I didn't get to actually eat my food until it was my husbands turn to drive, about three hours after we stopped, but it was still delicious.  This was definitely the most affordable place I ate at between md and ca but the quality was still top notch.  I'd prefer more fish per taco but I was so happy to get such a good amount of jalapeños in my pico de gallo.  Everything was very flavorful. Service was great and the restaurant itself was really nice inside too.  It's got that "hole in the wall" feel where you know you're about to get some awesome food without looking like a hole in the wall.  


Update:  I didn't think I'd be back since I was just traveling the first time.  But I'm stoked as hell.  I just got another order of fish tacos.  Still awesome.

I wouldn't come back here again. It was mediocre at best. 

We came here a lonnnng time ago. Decided to give it a try on a Saturday around 8:00. It was restaurant week. I didn't like the appetizer or dessert options so I opted to just order off of the regular menu. My fiancé ordered from the restaurant week menu and regretted it because he didn't like his appetizer or dessert so it wasn't really worth it. 

The tables inside are super on top of each other. I hate that so we dined outside. The tables are way too small. We are both tall so every time I tried to cross my legs under the table my knees would hit the table and shake it. I kept forgetting so it kept happening...every. Time. 

Our server did not introduce herself. She poured us water at first without saying anything. Eventually I asked her for a regular menu not knowing if she was just an assistant that brings water, clears tables, etc. or our actual waitress. Eventually we realized she was our waitress. She was nice and got the job done but the service was nothing to rave about. 

We filled out our card to customize our guacamole. It was very spicy. It was good but I've had better. A small portion for the price. Not enough chips...we had to ask for more. 

My fiancé got the ceviche which oddly enough wasn't served with anything to dip except for two small plantain chips. He said it was fishy. 

I thought the restaurant week menu was supposed to be the same size portions as the regular menu but I got veggie enchiladas and he got chicken enchiladas and my dish was served with 3, his with 2. My tomatillo sauce was good but the whole dish was cold. I asked what vegetables were in my veggie enchiladas but she said she didn't know and would have to ask. I didn't feel like waiting so I told her not to worry about it. I should have waited because I never would have ordered them in a million years. It was the strangest grouping of veggies I've ever gotten in a veggie enchilada. Broccoli, cauliflower, potatoes, zucchini. Ugh. Not good at all. Who puts potatoes in a veggie enchilada?!?

My fiancé got the tres leches cake for dessert. Again...nasty. It was the strangest texture ever. It tasted like rice pudding. Horrible. He regretted it and would never want to eat it again. 

The bathroom is soooooooo small. I mean, SMALL. like airplane bathroom small. I hated every second of being in it. It's just outside the kitchen, too. Bleh. 

We both agreed that we wouldn't have the desire to ever return here.

One of the best Karaoke bars I have EVER been to! Great for people of all ages. There is a main stage that you can sing on and feel like a professional or you can head out back to their outside stage for a little more of a private singing session.

Tucson staple. My dad took me here when I was, like, seventeen years old and made me order the "cabeza tacos." After I ate the tacos, which were stuffed with the most tender, melt in your mouth, delightful meat I have ever tasted, he broke the news that I had eaten "a beef head taco." Thanks, Dad (but it was delicious). Nowadays I don't really eat cow cheeks, although I do recommend anything "cabeza" to my carnivore friends and family. Now I just get bean burritos. Sounds boring, but at BK the bean burritos are OUT OF THIS WORLD! I just don't understand the prices. The burrito I got was pretty tiny and it was seven bucks. But worth it.

A few months ago I had my birthday here - rented one of their private rooms. The experience was spectacular!  The service was great, the room was awesome, the open bar was wonderful, the food was excellent and we were able to hook up our music to their sound system and had a little dance party!  It was very memorable and I highly recommend getting a private room and choosing a high end package as it is really worth it!  The room was setup perfectly with a big table in the center with beautiful linens, small tables on each end of the room, and then a few high top tables. Hydrangeas were at the center of each table.  And the floor to ceiling windows with view of south philly was gorgeous in the day light as well as at night. Taking a few steps to the bar made it easy to sip a cocktail while enjoying time with my company and the view. The butlered hors d' oeuvres kept coming and then my steak was cooked perfectly for my main entree!  Then came the desert and a lot of laughing and dancing with my family and friends !!
Great food and atmosphere. I had the fish and shrimp tacos. Perfection! Hubbs has the Carne Asada tacos. All food was good and service was good too. Guacamole was good too.

The hubby and I had a great time at R2L for dinner. He booked ahead for a south facing window table on 3/3/19 so we could get a gorgeous sun set dinner view. Unfortunately, that day it sleeted, cloud coverage and fog. I still enjoyed the food, the service was top notch, and the atmosphere was very romantic. Everyone from downstairs to hostess to server gave us a smile and was very polite and helpful. 

For our meal we were brought out 2 small bites from the kitchen which I believe was some type of salami with mustard topped with garlic confit and sliced almonds & micro greens over toasted slice of baguette; was delish! We also we brought out the standard bread, which were two cute little baguettes; funny part was it was on an IKEA cutting board. For drinks we both had water, and I got the "live long and..." and he got the Elder Fashion. They were both really good, his was nice n strong. We ordered the hot seafood boil appetizer, which was a nice portion, I could've just had that alone as my entrée. We loved the shishito peppers that came with it and have been obsessed ever since; and I highly recommend getting this and letting your mini baguette sop up all the liquid. Hubby got the 28day dry aged rib eye, I opted for the braised rib & filet duo, and we shared a side of crispy Brussels sprouts. I thought this wasn't going to be enough but we were at maximum capacity and had to take a small to go box home. Couldn't leave anything this good, or $$$ behind.  We did not have room for dessert but were offered by our server. 

During dinner, towards the end the sky did clear up and we were able to enjoy a beautiful night view of the city.  We had a lovely memorable dinner and will definitely return for another special occasion. 

Food:  5/5 everything came out to temp good portion & seasoned well
Atmosphere:  5/5 def a romantic date place/celebration dinner
Price: 4/5 a bit pricey if you ask me but you are paying for ambiance and service 
Service: 5/5 everyone

An absolute hidden gem. If you like latin food, you MUST MUST MUST check this spot out. WOW.

Fast, friendly service. Incredible selection. Buffet style.

CHECK IT OUT.

Very good burgers and our server was awesome.  I have passed this place for years and years and finally decided to give it a try when my mom was in town.  We were both very happy with our burgers and agreed we will be going back and should have given this place a try a lot sooner.  Yes, parking can be a bit tricky at times as there are only a few spots in front but once you carefully drive around back, there were some open spots.
Loved the food here, my first experience with the food of this culture. Very flavorful dishes in a unique yet cozy setting. Looking forward to returning soon!

Be alerted - you may pay the same as the luxiourios   Cinemark but don't be fooled, you are not getting the same quality accommodations by any means. Average seats, nothing more than most theaters.  Seriously disappointed - would've driven the extra 4 miles for comfort at the same price. Except there is a bar now but at assuredly at excessive pricing based on the occupancy and the high cost of movie tickets
Enjoyed the view. Food and service at the table was good but trying to get a drink at the almost empty bar was a struggle!  Bartender looked right through us.

A friend recommended this place. We had never been to a Peruvian restaurant. The atmosphere was perfect!  The service was great! The food was truly delicious!  They have a huge wine list with wines from all over the world!! I had the Lomo Saltado as recommended by the waitress and it was perfect!  My fiancé had a Bistec a Lo Pobre which he loved! (Pic included).  We loved the "green" rice!  The plates were well presented and we all enjoyed our dinner, total 6 of us! We already recommended to friends and family and will be returning!

Vietnamese of this sort is basically a commodity, and Pho An Hoa does an admirable job at this staple.  It is not remarkable or especially notable, and the execution here is just fine and what you would expect.  I would like it more and definitely go if it was my neighborhood spot.  There were plenty of ethnic folks here, which is always a mark of authenticity and quality, and the food here is made with love.  But there is really nothing unique to distinguish this place from the many, and to my mind better, competitors. 

I had my regular fare: fresh rolls, followed by rare beef pho with added meatballs.  It was good, and the prices and portions were more than reasonable.  

Thank you for a lovely meal.  I would be back if I am ever in the neighborhood.
The stand to be seat took almost 5 minute.  The servers was just walking around before finally come and offer us a seat.  I ordered egg Benedict they put cheese on the hollandaise sauce.

I went there for dinner on Monday night. I think they recently got license for full bar. Ordered two different cocktails both of them were in drinkable. Pineapple infused were very salty ... there spicy drink was so spicy one sip of it hurt my throat so bad. Food was ordinary... I only like fried rice ...

The view, beautiful, the Staff friendly and pleasant, the food, delicious. My Co-Workers and I decided to have dinner at this restaurant due to it being  Center City Restaurant Week.  We enjoyed pleasant conversation along with a calming  atmosphere. I will consider returning with the Hubby. I observed a few romantic corners.

Wowww! This place has been on my list to try for a while. We finally made it out this past Sunday night around 7pm. The restaurant was busy but we were sat right away. It took a little bit for our server to come over, but again, it was pretty packed.

We ordered 3 pizzas -- pepperoni, white, and square margherita. The pepperoni was hands down something that I will crave for weeks and months to come. I didn't want to stop eating it! The white pizza had an amazing cheese ratio but it could've had a little more garlic and ricotta on it. The margherita was exceptional as well but didn't compare to the pepperoni.

Somehow we saved enough room to order one of each dessert -- cheesecake, mini cannolis, and chocolate cake. The chocolate cake was actually really dry and not my favorite. Cannolis were delicious but that cheesecake was everything!! I'm not sure why but the after taste was reminiscent of rice pudding (which I loved!). It was one of my favorite cheesecakes. Classic yet unique.

The pepperoni pizza and the cheesecake were 5-star-worthy but I did dock the review 1 star because the other items were on the more average side. We also had to ask for drink refills multiple times. I will 100% recommend to anyone and I will go back, especially now that I really know what to order. I just saw reviews about the wings so I'm excited to give those a shot when I go back.
Mediocre food, drinks are fair to ok.  The service here is awful.  Not the place we expected to be a great cocktail bar as described by others.

Generally my family and i enjoy the food at eats but last night the restaurant looked and smelled bad. Almost like the grease hadnt been changed in a while.  The tables are so old they are sticky, the paper menus were tattered and the floor carpets looked dirty and black. I dont know if mgt has changed but it looked shabby which made me question the cleanliness of the kitchen. All the food seemed fine but i think they need to spruce up the place before i come back.

There's an easy wow factor to these tacos. The corn tortillas are perfect. Flavorful, a little crunchy, but soft on the inside. The fish and shrimp are absolutely delicious. I'm from San Diego where we take pride in fish and shrimp tacos and these are just as good as what you'll find there. The rice and beans are all really good too. The salsa has a great roasted flavor and doesn't hold back on any spice. Perfect for my tastes.
The drinks were good. It was a nice atmosphere. I just wish the food portions were bigger

I am actually confused at some of the lesser ratings. If you don't get the pricey special rolls and just go for normal rolls or sushi, you can't go wrong. This is the best place i have had sushi in this area, period. Better than blue fin, better than Oishi and many others The fish is extremely fresh, which to me, in a place in a strip mall is all i care about. The quality of the fish is far superior to any place i have been in this area (philly and the burbs). I have no issues with the salads or the soups either. And if you order a $20 roll, please note they are huge and you really don't need much more than that anyway. I also enjoy the attention from the employees. I  live in Bucks County and this is the only sushi place I get excited to go to and have no issues driving past a dozen or so other places to go here.

Update - I changed this to a five star after my visit tonight. Service from the server to the sushi chef to the manager (maybe he's the owner?) was fantastic. Recommendations were spot on and the quality of salmon and other fish is just top notch compared to places near me in yardley and Newtown and elsewhere. This is my favorite sushi place around by leaps and bounds - still.

First things first, it is $25 per person. All tables are served the same food (aka no menu, you eat what you're served -- but you do have the choice of meat and can mention dietary restrictions i.e. gluten free).

The night starts with everyone in the group putting their hands in the middle of the table, over a bowl, as a waiter pours rose water over everyones hands. This is their way of washing hands - as you are about to eat your meal with no utensils!!! Yes, the entire meal is meant to be eaten with your hands. However, if you can't eat without a fork/knife, the staff can provide you with them.

Seems as though there are two different seatings. When making the reservation, they said availability was only 6-6:30 or 9-9:30. I like this idea of having a time window for being seated.

A belly dancer gave a two song performance during the night - great entertainment!

The food includes pita bread, vegetable appetizer, a couple of meat platters, fruit platter, and finally a dessert platter.

When we received the bill, it seemed a bit high. The waiter, while  stumbling over his words, said the corkage fee was $18, $6/bottle. This was a shock to us as all Yelp reviews said there is no corkage fee (and the waiters didn't mention it when they opened our bottles). Disappointed by that.

ALSO - IT'S CASH ONLY!!!! Come prepared.

One star off for corkage fee miscommunication and we felt a bit rushed.

Intimate setting, amazing wine selection (100 wines by the glass), delicious food -- ceviche is on point and best paella ever, mushroom fried rice is excellent. Anthony was a great server. Highly recommend this awesome local restaurant!

I frequent this restaurant often. From their breakfast menu, to lunch and dinner, they offer an array of Latin cuisines. All food is served hot and fresh. Although lines are long, they have a system in place to get customers in and out in no time. Local favorite.

Awesome selection of beer, great food, nice people, great environment. Came for lunch and a beer, had the Mojo Pork Tacos and OMG they are amazing. Definitely get the sauce that comes on it. I had it on the side because it is "sour cream based" and I don't like sour cream, but the sauce is totally amazing. Also the Cuban bread is a delicious snack (to soak up the yummy beer!!)

Definitely give the Independent a try!

Not the LaScala's it used to be. At one time, it was a go-to restaurant for me. The atmosphere, food, and service was great. Now, I'm giving 1 star for service, and 1 star for the apps. What's good... the service. Our waiter was Kenardi, and LaScala's is lucky to have him. He's the only reason the experience didn't end up being a disaster. He's very attentive and friendly. The food... The bread and oil dip is excellent. The appetizers we had was pretty good (White Pear Pizza, especially ) and Im glad because we sent back 2 entrees. Both were chicken pasta dishes. The chicken was literally boiled in water and thrown on top of noodles, it was absolutely flavorless, the sauce was just as bad... it was like eating cardboard topped with chalk. Both dishes were really terrible. I couldn't even dress it up. Use the seasoning rack, dammit! The atmosphere is confusing. They have candles lit on all the tables, but what mood is that setting when your customers under lights bright enough for a broadway show. Also, they don't pay attention to seating. The restaurant was pretty empty, so sitting adult parties next a table with 3 screaming kids wasn't very thoughtful. I think it's unfortunate the way LaScala's has changed. It's not hard to get it right, you just have to pay attention, and put the effort in. Prior to this visit, it has been a long time. I'm not gonna say I'll never go back, because I'm really hoping this was just a bad night, and I can still count on one of my old favorite restaurants. We'll see.
Was pleasantly surprised..the food was very tasty and my waiter was very good!! Will tell friends to try it and am glad I know this is there!!

This is our favorite place to lunch on Sunday's. We bring the dog too! They have great food and a nice selection of beers both on tap as well. They also serve wine. My favorite sandwich is the Rob. Every once in a while I'll have the Reuben or the Cuban. Staff is engaging and very good at finding you the right beer.

I have not tasted mass so freshly ground in the green chile tamale since my travels in Mexico almost fifty years ago.  The aromatic sweetness and texture of the main hits on all cylinders.  The rice is the traditional mid grain of high quality that is neither salty nor greasy.  It is the neutral compliment to the rest of the meal offerings.  The home made corn tortillas are thick and freshly made.  There is a distinct nuttiness to masa that has not been refrigerated.  This is the real thing just in time. When reheated from room temperature the masa blooms once again and creates a combustible delicacy with whatever meat or sauces fills the tortilla.  Because the ingredients are such great quality and not processed each bite is a treasure trove of satisfying culinary pleasure.  I suggest everyone ordering different items with extra tortillas and sharing a thick slice of flan.  I just had a can of Coke after remembering a half a century's old  Mexican advertising jingle . Coca Cola es la Chispa de la Vida'  You will feel the sparkle the minute you see the colorful exterior and not want to quickly return to the otherwise bland street outside.  Pico De Gallo is the Sparkle of Life!
